data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You self-scope from the recommendation, roadmap, and opened inbox below.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level: experiment, change the strategy entirely, try alternative formulations, edit blueprint material when proving teaches you something, and — when a task calls for it — work across the several projects it spans, or create and merge projects. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the projects in scope and the shared `references/`, don't leave stray directories or scratch files behind. The Ground agent and its review subagents run after you to reconcile, check math/blueprint alignment, and tidy — so prefer making progress over being cautious.

How to work:
- Read the relevant blueprint node and proof sketch before touching Lean.
- Run the project's build/check command (or the narrowest useful Lean check) and repair failures you introduce. Don't bury obligations under new `sorry`s unless a hint allows it.
- Use the inbox to comment, close, create, and message other projects with `--author horizon` (see the horizon-inbox skill). ALWAYS write item bodies, comments, and reports in Markdown — short paragraphs and `-` bullet lists (never one wall-of-text paragraph), `**bold**` for the key claim, and backticks for ids, lemma names, and files. Give every item a short title and a non-empty description. Comment at each key advance, and add a concise closing comment (runs, LOC, files) before you `complete` an item. Treat `[persistent]` items as standing rules; close `[temporary]` ones once consumed.
- When your work completes or blocks a task/roadmap milestone, leave a concise task or roadmap comment with the decisive checks, changed files, and remaining caveats. If a task has linked `roadmap_refs`, keep your report/comment explicit about the status each linked roadmap item should have so Ground or the human can keep the roadmap consistent.
- Record durable dead ends as memory: `horizon inbox add --kind memory --to horizon --author horizon --body "..."`, so later sessions don't repeat them.
- You may delegate to subagents when it helps (see the Subagents section): split a wide search across parallel read-only subagents, hand mechanical checks to a cheaper-model subagent, or pull references. You are free to decide whether they fit the task.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, close, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting — How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session — block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions in mathlib, workspace projects, and configured libraries with `horizon search`; use external Mathlib search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/Moogle/LSP) when broader Mathlib search is needed before writing proofs.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: .archon-horizon/runs/0005/sessions/0015-horizon-T5

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

# Latest Ground recommendation
This was saved as `recommendation.md` by the last Ground session; treat it as fresh guidance, not as a hard command if the live state contradicts it.
## Next

- Launch `AJC.pic0av` → `tangentSpaceIso` (`Dual_k(m_e/m_e²) ≅ H¹(C,O_C)`) — the unblocked frontier.
- Human: decide `Picard-IdentityComponent` mirror re-sync vs retirement, and RR.4 scratch descope.

# Recommended focus
Task: T5
Title: Extract & prove Pic⁰ IdentityComponent (FBC-independent picrep spine)
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Suggested scope: projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge; files=AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/IdentityComponent*.lean

Advance the Picard-representability (picrep) spine WITHOUT depending on flat base change (FBC/02KH), so this runs fully in parallel with the live FBC run (T2) in AJC.

STEP 0 (gate — do this first, report before proceeding): Verify that the `Picard/IdentityComponent.lean` import cone is genuinely FBC/cohomology-independent. Trace its transitive imports in MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge. If the cone pulls in the Čech/cohomology base-change engine (Cohomology/Cech*, the 02KH flat-base-change modules), STOP and report — do not extract; instead reduce scope to only the FBC-free declarations. If clean, continue.

STEP 1 (extraction — own build dir, MUST NOT build the AJC tree): Create a NEW standalone Lean subproject (e.g. SubProjects/Picard-IdentityComponent) with its own lakefile.toml (require mathlib rev v4.31.0, packagesDir ../../.lake-packages), lean-toolchain leanprover/lean4:v4.31.0, and its own .lake — mirroring the Albanese/GR-Quot-Closure extraction pattern. Copy in `Picard/IdentityComponent.lean` and the minimal import cone needed to build it. Register it with `horizon project add` (enable vcs git_dir). The point of a separate subproject is build isolation: it must NOT touch or rebuild the AJC .lake that T2 is using.

STEP 2 (prove): Close the 9 structural sorries in IdentityComponent — Pic⁰ as the open-and-closed connected-component subgroup functor and the abelian-group-structure (Yoneda) transport — the parts that do NOT need the cohomology / flat-base-change engine. Leave any sorry that genuinely depends on FBC or full FGA representability documented and untouched, with a note on the blocker.

DELIVERABLE: the new subproject builds GREEN (lake build EXIT=0) with the FBC-free IdentityComponent sorries closed and axiom-clean; blueprint/DAG updated; it is positioned to merge back into AJC sorry-free like Albanese/GR. Reads from the AJC tree as source.

# Roadmap slice
- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Čech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **Čech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC (axiom-clean). Residual 16 `sorry` are v4.31 migration-interim, mechanical.

- AJC.fbc [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), Čech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). Two open leaves: `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` (Stacks 02KG, termwise affine BC via `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact). See task **T2**.

- AJC.grquot [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged) - **Relative Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged, sorry-free).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman §5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley–Rosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.

- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (Kähler, rigidity, Abel–Jacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** Kähler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, Abel–Jacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).


# Opened inbox
- I-0053 info (by=ground, labels=agent-ready, projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge): Diff audit run 0005 T5 (commit 6e7f7ae): PASS, no defects. Verified: - IdentityComponent.isSubgroupHomomorphism + IdentityComponent.baseChangeIso genuinely closed. Kernel #print axioms = [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound] for both — no sorryAx. Helper chain (identityComponentSubgroupFunctor/RepresentableBy, identityComponent_geometricallyConnected via geometricallyConnected_of_connected_of_section) is sorry-free. - No buried sorry/admit/native_decide/new axiom anywhere in the +1103 added lines (all textual 'sorry' hits are in docstrings: 'sorry-free', 'typed-sorry'). - IdentityComponent.lean now has exactly 6 genuine code sorries (796 isFiniteTypeGeometricallyIrreducible; 995/1036/1089…

# Memory
- v4.31.0 migration fix patterns (Cech + GR push-pull/pullback proofs)

Effective repairs for the **v4.31.0 defeq-transparency-wall** breaks in these projects:

- **Port from the AJC copy first.** `MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/.../<same file>` is often already sorry-free (e.g. `PresheafCech.lean`, `CechSectionIdentificationBase.lean`). `diff` the subproject vs AJC; if they differ only in the sorry regions, copy the AJC file wholesale.
- **`set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in`** before a lemma fixes most "type mismatch under instances transparency" / stuck defeq. **Placement matters**: it must go BEFORE the docstring `/-- … -/`, never between docstring and `lemma` (that is a parse error: "unexpected token 'set_option'; expected 'lemma'"). Stacks with `set_option maxHeartbeats N in`.
- **`erw [Category.comp_id]` / `erw [Category.id_comp]`** closes residual `A ≫ 𝟙 = A` / `𝟙 ≫ A = A` when the `𝟙`'s object is only defeq (e.g. `(𝟭).obj M` vs `M`, or a `⋙`-form) so `simp`/`exact Category.comp_id _` fail. `erw` matches through the wall then auto-`rfl`.
- **`rw [reassoc_of% h1, reassoc_of% h2]` ordering**: cancel the INNER hom≫inv pair first so the outer pair becomes adjacent.
- **`subst h; simp <;> rfl`** for eqToHom-transport lemmas whose two sides reduce to defeq-but-not-syntactic `map = map`.
- **LSP is dead** on these full-`import Mathlib` files (server crashes / broken pipe). Iterate with `lake build <module>` and read the printed unsolved-goal state from the error output. `GlueDescent` ≈ 2.6 min/build; `CechHigherDirectImage` ≈ 8.5 min/build.
- **SOLVED (supersedes the earlier "two v4.31 sorrys remain hard" memory): both `rawPushPullMap_comp` (Cech) and `pullback_cast_compat` (GR) are now proved sorry-free.**

- **`CechHigherDirectImage.lean` `rawPushPullMap_comp`**: final transport is pure **term-mode**, no `rw` (avoids "motive not type correct"). Shape: `(congrArg (fun t => (pushforward p₁).map A_head ≫ t) he).trans (((Functor.map_comp _ _ _).symm.trans (congrArg (pushforward p₁).map ((Category.assoc _ _ _).trans INNER))).trans (Functor.map_comp _ _ _))`. `he` rewrites the outer over-triangle `eqToHom` to `(pushforward p₁).map eqInner`; `Category.assoc` fixes `(x≫y)≫z` vs `x≫(y≫z)` before `INNER`; `map_comp` folds/splits.

- **`GlueDescent.lean` `pullback_cast_compat`**: proved from the associativity solved-forms. Recipe: (1) `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` BEFORE the docstring (the `pullbackComp` solved-forms unfold to the `⋙`-object form; else v4.31 transparency wall blocks matching). (2) tagged `pullbackComp_inv_comp_map_inv_app` + `pullbackComp_inv_comp_congr_hom_app` `@[reassoc]`. (3) phase-1 `simp only [pullbackComp_comp_fst_hom_app, pullback_map_inv_comp_hom_app_assoc, pullback_map_congr_inv_comp_hom_app_assoc, pullbackComp_inv_comp_congr_hom_app_assoc, pullbackComp_inv_comp_map_inv_app(_assoc), Category.assoc]` keeping congr as casts. (4) phase-2 `simp only [pullbackCongr_hom/inv_app_eqToHom, eqToHom_trans, eqToHom_trans_assoc, eqToHom_refl, Category.assoc, comp_id, id_comp]` (need `eqToHom_trans_assoc` for mid-chain). Variance: `pullbackComp x y : pullback y ⋙ pullback x ≅ pullback (x≫y)`.

- Iterate via `lake build <module>` reading the unsolved-goal state (LSP dead).
- **Subproject merge-back recipe (worked for T1 / Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso → AJC; reuse for Albanese / Cech back-merges).**

- **Wholesale lift beats cherry-pick** when the subproject built green on the same toolchain+mathlib rev: replace every diverged shared file with the subproject copy, add the new files, then repair AJC-only consumers. Verify first with a decl-name superset check (regex-extract `def|lemma|...` heads, `comm -23`) and a consumer grep for the decls that moved/disappeared.
- **Check for cross-module duplicate decl names BEFORE building**: two modules both imported by the root that declare the same fully-qualified name are a fatal clash. In T1, LBC's `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` had absorbed 13 decls of AJC's `PullbackTensorComp.lean` (incl. `pullbackTensorMap_restrict`) → the right fix was to **retire the superseded module** (its 8 unique helpers had no consumers).
- **Blueprint: port per-chapter, not per-project.** Wholesale-copy a chapter only after a comment-stripped cross-ref check: (refs of incoming chapter) ⊆ (its labels ∪ other AJC chapters' labels), and no other AJC chapter references a disappearing label. In T1 the TOS chapter ported cleanly; LBC's restructured `RelPicFunctor`/`LineBundlePullback` chapters did NOT (6 `\cref` sites in `Picard_FGAPicRepresentability.tex` would dangle) — kept AJC's copies. Their reconciliation is optional future work.
- Big-file build times on this box: `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` (4592 L) + `TensorObjInverse.lean` (3424 L) + rest of the cone ≈ 15 min wall with warm mathlib cache.
- FBC bareBC Stage-2 patterns + scratch-iteration gotchas (session 0003-T2)

**Mate-factorization pattern (landed, reusable for other Beck-Chevalley mates):**

- `mateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α |>.app c` factors as `adj₂.unit.app _ ≫ R₂.map (α.app _) ≫ R₂.map (H.map (adj₁.counit.app c))`. Proof: `simp [<def>, mateEquiv_apply]` then `erw [Category.id_comp, Category.id_comp, Category.comp_id]; rfl` (three defeq-blocked `𝟙`s + one defeq head).
- Counit of `pullback q ⊣ pushforward q` at open immersion `q` is iso: `IsIso.of_isIso_fac_left (Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq_hom_app_counit (restrictAdjunction q) (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction q) c)` with `haveI` on `(restrictAdjunction q).counit` iso via `inferInstanceAs (IsIso (restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso q).hom)`.
- essImage → unit-iso: `Adjunction.isIso_unit_app_of_iso` (needs `pushforward` Full+Faithful — Mathlib instances for open immersions). Reverse `mem_essImage_of_unit_isIso`.
- **essImage of `p'_*` is cover-local** (`essImage_pushforward_of_openCover`, now in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional`): site-level `restrictAdjunction` unit has plain `M.presheaf.map homOfLE` components; `isIso_iff_isIso_restrict` + `Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app` need NO push/pull coherence — components of the restricted map are again plain presheaf maps; match via `w''(O ⊓ w⁻¹R) = R ⊓ w''O` (`Set.image_inter_preimage`) + one `eqToHom (congrArg Opposite.op h)` factor. Poset-hom equalities: `congr 1` after `rw [← Functor.map_comp]` discharges by proof-irrelevance; op-level casts via `Quiver.Hom.unop_inj; Subsingleton.elim`.

**Scratch-iteration gotchas:**

- `lake env lean scratch.lean` does NOT apply lakefile `leanOptions`. Add `set_option maxSynthPendingDepth 3` at scratch top or instance chains through functor categories fail mysteriously (e.g. `IsIso ((leftAdjointUniq ..).hom.app M)` composites). Destination files under `lake build` are fine.
- Mathlib-only lemmas iterate in ~3 min via a root-level scratch importing `Mathlib` (+ light project modules whose oleans exist) — 20x faster than the project file. Delete scratch before commit.
- `CechSectionIdentificationLegMid1.lean` takes ~70+ min to compile (`maxHeartbeats 0`, documented in-file); it IS in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional`'s import cone (via `CechTermAcyclic → CechSectionIdentificationBase → … `). Full verification of Unconditional edits costs hours if its olean is stale/missing.
- Beware RACING concurrent lake builds of the same project from leftover/parallel sessions (two `lean` processes on the same file observed) — olean corruption risk, duplicated 70-min work.
- Recipe: Stacks 00OE/00TT closed-point regularity for standard-smooth algebras (v4.31 Mathlib suffices)

**The smooth-algebra Krull-dimension formula at maximal ideals needs NO transcendence-degree theory** — do not build Noether-normalisation dimension theory for it. Proven route (Albanese `StandardSmoothDimension.lean` + `CodimOneExtension.lean` §3.B, all axiom-clean):

- `ht M = #ι` for maximal `M ⊆ MvPolynomial ι k`: `Finite.induction_empty_option` (works with hypotheses depending on `ι` — the tactic auto-generalises `M`, `hM`); step = `Polynomial.isMaximal_comap_C_of_isJacobsonRing` (contraction maximal, `MvPolynomial.isJacobsonRing` instance exists for `Finite ι`) + `Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one`; transport via `RingEquiv.height_map/height_comap` + `Ideal.map/comap_isMaximal_of_equiv`.
- Lower bound `n ≤ ht m` in standard-smooth `S`: pull `m` back along `P.algebraMap_surjective` of the submersive presentation; `Ideal.height_le_height_add_spanFinrank_of_le` (Krull height thm, relative form) + `spanFinrank(ker) ≤ #σ` via `P.span_range_relation_eq_ker`; quotient side identified with `m` via `RingHom.quotientKerEquivOfSurjective_apply_mk` + `Ideal.map_comap_of_surjective`; cancel in ℕ∞ with `ENat.add_le_add_iff_right`.
- Upper bound is free: `IsRegularLocalRing.of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le` + `IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace`.
- k̄-rationality of maximal ideals: `finite_of_finite_type_of_isJacobsonRing` (Zariski) + `IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral` + `Ideal.bijective_algebraMap_quotient_residueField`. **Trap:** install `letI := Ideal.Quotient.field m` BEFORE any other `haveI` on `S ⧸ m` (FiniteType, Module.Finite) — installing it after poisons `Algebra k (S ⧸ m)` synthesis (semiring-key mismatch; `respectTransparency false` does NOT fix it; Field-first ordering does).

**SUPERSEDED for the all-points case (run 0006 T6): Stacks 00OF is NO LONGER needed.** The old claim here — "remaining genuine gap for all-points `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` is Stacks 00OF, do not attempt" — is now false. See [[I-0047]]: `Albanese/SmoothPrimeRegularity.lean` proves regularity at EVERY prime of a standard-smooth algebra over a perfect field, Serre-free (trdeg–height induction via Stacks 00ON + conormal split-exactness from `FormallySmooth.of_perfectField`), so `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` / `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` are now axiom-clean. This closed-point recipe is retained only for its k̄-rationality trap and the maximal-ideal API details.
- **FBC member-node patterns (session 0007-T2) — the essImage route that closed `IsIso bareBC`.**

- **Specialize the Beck–Chevalley square to `p = V₀.ι` (`V₀ : X.Opens`, `IsAffineOpen V₀`)** instead of an abstract affine open immersion `p : V ⟶ X`. Every consumer instantiates that form (`coverInterOpen`), and it makes qcoh bookkeeping FREE via iterated `isQuasicoherent_pullback_opens` — no general 'pullback along open immersion preserves qcoh' needed (that exists only in sorry-tainted `QuotScheme.lean`, do NOT import it).
- **Prove 'pullback of pushforward is a pushforward' as essImage membership, with the ring square the ABSTRACT pushout** `B := pushout φ ψ` (`IsPushout.of_hasPushout`): geometric cartesianness is free (`isPullback_SpecMap_of_isPushout`), `Spec σ` open-immersion by stability, its range via `IsOpenImmersion.range_pullbackSnd` + `hpb.isoPullback_hom_snd` + `Set.range_eq_univ.mpr h.surjective`, and the final match with the preimage open by `IsOpenImmersion.isoOfRangeEq` + `pushforwardComp/Congr`. NO four-corner isoSpec transport of a concrete square.
- **`pullback e.hom ≅ pushforward e.inv`** (scheme iso e): `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction e.hom) (pushforwardEquivOfIso e).symm.toAdjunction` — one-liner, no defeq wall.

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.

4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.IdentityComponent
8	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.TangentSpaceDualNumbers
9	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.TangentSpaceIdentitySection
10	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus
11	
12	/-!
13	# `Pic⁰_{C/k}` as an abelian variety (A.3.iii–vii)
14	
15	This file is the **iter-193 Pic0AbelianVariety** file-skeleton (NEW lane,
16	blueprint-doctor flagged orphan resolution for the iter-192 plan-phase chapter
17	`Picard_Pic0AbelianVariety.tex`). It packages the strategy phase A.3.iii–vi
18	substrate and the A.3.vii assembly into the load-bearing Route~A statement
19	`Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety, the gate of `thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness`
20	for `g(C) ≥ 1` curves.
21	
22	The chapter consumes the abstract identity-component substrate set up in
23	`Picard/IdentityComponent.lean` (`Pic⁰_{C/k}` as a clopen subgroup scheme of
24	finite type, geometrically irreducible, formation commuting with base change)
25	and adds the curve-specific input: the tangent-space isomorphism
26	`T₀ Pic⁰_{C/k} ≅ H¹(C, 𝒪_C)`, the resulting smoothness of dimension `g` at
27	the origin (hence everywhere by translation), properness inherited from the
28	projectivity of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` for `C/k` geometrically normal, and the assembly
29	into an abelian variety in the sense of Milne §I.1.
30	
31	## Status (iter-193 NEW file-skeleton)
32	
33	Each of the five blueprint-pinned declarations carries the *intended*
34	substantive type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
35	`Picard_Pic0AbelianVariety.tex`) with a `sorry` body. The bodies are
36	iter-194+ work; iter-193's mandate is the mechanical scaffold only.
37	
38	The 5 pinned declarations are:
39	
40	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0.tangentSpaceIso` (theorem, A.3.iii) — the
41	   canonical isomorphism `T₀ Pic⁰_{C/k} ≅ H¹(C, 𝒪_C)` (Kleiman §5
42	   Thm.~`thm:tgtsp`). Packaged as an `AddEquiv` between the cotangent space
43	   `m_e/m_e²` at a `k`-rational identity-section point `e` and the project's
44	   first-cohomology `k`-module `Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1`
45	   (from `Genus.lean`). The `k`-module structure on the cotangent space at a
46	   `k`-rational point is supplied as part of the existential bundle.
47	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0.smooth` (theorem, A.3.iv) — `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is
48	   `Smooth` over `k`. Kleiman §5 Cor.~`cor:sm` + Cor.~`cor:ch0`
49	   (characteristic-zero) + Ex.~`ex:jac` (curve case).
50	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0.proper` (theorem, A.3.v) — `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is
51	   `IsProper` over `k`. Kleiman §5 Thm.~`th:qpp&p` (projectivity upgrade for
52	   geometrically normal `C/k`).
53	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0.geometricallyIrreducible` (theorem, A.3.vi)
54	   — `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is `GeometricallyIrreducible` over `k`. Specialisation of
55	   `IdentityComponent.isFiniteTypeGeometricallyIrreducible` (sibling) to
56	   `G = PicScheme C`. Kleiman §5 Prp.~`prp:pic0`.
57	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0.isAbelianVariety` (theorem, A.3.vii) —
58	   `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety: `IsProper ∧ Smooth ∧
59	   GeometricallyIrreducible ∧ Nonempty (GrpObj _)`. Milne §I.1, p.~8
60	   (defining axioms) + Kleiman §5 Rmk.~`rmk:Jac`. Load-bearing A.3.vii gate
61	   of Route~A.
62	
63	## Note on type expressivity
64	
65	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof", each
66	pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
67	
68	- `tangentSpaceIso C` — existentially quantifies over a `k`-rational
69	  identity-section point `e : Spec k ⟶ (Pic0Scheme C).left` and a `k`-module
70	  structure on the cotangent space `m_e/m_e²` (the `k`-module structure
71	  arising via the `k`-algebra structure on the stalk at a `k`-rational point;
72	  for the file-skeleton this Module instance is bundled as part of the Σ').
73	  Bundles an `AddEquiv` between the cotangent space and the
74	  `H¹(C, 𝒪_C)`-as-`k`-module. The body is iter-194+ work.
75	- `smooth C`, `proper C`, `geometricallyIrreducible C` — genuine `Prop`-valued
76	  statements on the structural morphism `(Pic0Scheme C).hom : Pic⁰_{C/k} ⟶
77	  Spec k`, not tautological.
78	- `isAbelianVariety C` — assembles the conjunction of the four
79	  abelian-variety properties (proper, smooth, geometrically irreducible,
80	  group-object structure); not vacuous because each conjunct is a genuine
81	  property/structure on the (typed-sorry) `Pic0Scheme C`.
82	
83	## Coordination with `Pic0Scheme`
84	
85	The underlying `k`-scheme `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is supplied by
86	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0Scheme C` from sibling
87	`Picard/IdentityComponent.lean` (`def:pic_zero_subscheme` in the blueprint).
88	We do not redefine the underlying scheme here; the `Pic0` namespace below
89	collects the curve-specific abelian-variety facts about `Pic0Scheme C`.
90	
91	## References
92	
93	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_Pic0AbelianVariety.tex`
94	(735 LOC, 5 pins). Sources:
95	- Kleiman, "The Picard scheme", §5, Thm.~`thm:tgtsp` (tangent space),
96	  Cor.~`cor:sm` + Cor.~`cor:ch0` (smoothness), Thm.~`th:qpp&p`
97	  (quasi-projectivity/projectivity), Prp.~`prp:pic0` (irreducibility),
98	  Rmk.~`rmk:Jac` (assembly to abelian scheme); arXiv:math/0504020.
99	- Milne, "Abelian Varieties" (course notes, 2008), §I.1, p.~8
100	  (definition of abelian variety) + Rmk. III.1.4(e) (dimension equals genus).
101	-/
102	
103	set_option autoImplicit false
104	
105	universe u
106	
107	open CategoryTheory Limits
108	
109	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
110	
111	namespace Scheme
112	
113	/-! ## §1. The `Pic0` namespace
114	
115	The blueprint chapter pins five Lean declarations under the
116	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0` namespace. Their underlying scheme
117	`Pic⁰_{C/k}` is `Pic0Scheme C` from sibling `Picard/IdentityComponent.lean`;
118	the declarations below collect the curve-specific abelian-variety facts
119	about `Pic0Scheme C` (tangent space at the identity, smoothness, properness,
120	geometric irreducibility, abelian-variety assembly). -/
121	
122	namespace Pic0
123	
124	/-- **Dual-number points of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` at the identity are the cotangent
125	dual** (Kleiman §5 Thm.~`thm:tgtsp`, LHS). Given the `k`-group-scheme
126	structure on `Pic0Scheme C` (supplied by
127	`GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isSubgroupHomomorphism` once `Pic0Scheme`
128	unwinds to `IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)`), the identity section
129	`e : Spec k ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k}` hits a `k`-rational point, and the over-`Spec k`
130	dual-number points of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` at `e` — the Zariski tangent space
131	`T₀ Pic⁰_{C/k}` in its functor-of-points form — form the `κ(e)`-linear dual
132	of the cotangent space `m_e/m_e²`.
133	
134	This is the geometric half of `tangentSpaceIso`; composing with the
135	`H¹(C, 𝒪_C)`-identification of `Dual (m_e/m_e²)` (gated on the `AJC.picrep`
136	representability cone) closes `thm:pic0_tangent_space_iso`. -/
137	theorem tangentSpaceCotangentDual {k : Type u} [Field k]
138	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
139	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
140	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom]
141	    (hgrp : Nonempty (GrpObj (Pic0Scheme C))) :
142	    Nonempty (Σ' (e : Spec (.of k) ⟶ (Pic0Scheme C).left),
143	      (e ≫ (Pic0Scheme C).hom = 𝟙 (Spec (.of k))) ×'
144	      ({g : Spec (CommRingCat.of (DualNumber k)) ⟶ (Pic0Scheme C).left //
145	          g ≫ (Pic0Scheme C).hom
146	              = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap k (DualNumber k)))
147	            ∧ g.base (IsLocalRing.closedPoint (DualNumber k)) = e.base default} ≃
148	        Module.Dual
149	          (IsLocalRing.ResidueField ((Pic0Scheme C).left.presheaf.stalk (e.base default)))
150	          (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace
151	            ((Pic0Scheme C).left.presheaf.stalk (e.base default))))) := by
152	  obtain ⟨i⟩ := hgrp
153	  letI := i
154	  haveI : Subsingleton ↥(Spec (CommRingCat.of k)) :=
155	    inferInstanceAs (Subsingleton (PrimeSpectrum k))
156	  exact ⟨GroupScheme.identitySection (Pic0Scheme C),
157	    GroupScheme.identitySection_comp (Pic0Scheme C),
158	    overDualNumberSectionEquivCotangentSpaceDual (Pic0Scheme C)
159	      (GroupScheme.identitySection_comp (Pic0Scheme C))
160	      (congrArg _ (Subsingleton.elim _ _))⟩
161	
162	/-- **Tangent space at the identity: `T₀ Pic⁰_{C/k} ≅ H¹(C, 𝒪_C)`.**
163	
164	The Kleiman §5 Thm.~`thm:tgtsp` tangent-space isomorphism. For a smooth
165	proper geometrically integral curve `C/k`, the Zariski tangent space at the
166	identity of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is canonically isomorphic to the first sheaf
167	cohomology `H¹(C, 𝒪_C)`.
168	
169	In Lean (skeleton form): we bundle existentially over a `k`-rational
170	identity-section point `e : Spec k ⟶ (Pic0Scheme C).left` and a `k`-module
171	structure on the cotangent space `m_e/m_e²` (the natural `k`-module structure
172	arising from the `k`-algebra structure on the stalk at a `k`-rational point;
173	for the file-skeleton this instance is supplied via Σ' to avoid hard-coding
174	a global instance prematurely). The substantive content is an `AddEquiv`
175	between the cotangent space and the project's `H¹(C, 𝒪_C)` module
176	`Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1`.
177	
178	A k-LinearEquiv refinement (iter-194+) replaces the `AddEquiv` once the
179	`k`-module structure on the cotangent space is consistently threaded through
180	downstream consumers; the underlying additive group structure is enough for
181	the dimension corollary `dim_k T₀ Pic⁰ = dim_k H¹ = g(C)`. -/
182	theorem tangentSpaceIso {k : Type u} [Field k]
183	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
184	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
185	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
186	    Nonempty (Σ' (e : Spec (.of k) ⟶ (Pic0Scheme C).left),
187	      IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace ((Pic0Scheme C).left.presheaf.stalk (e.base default))
188	        ≃+ Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1) :=
189	  sorry
190	
191	/-- **Smoothness of `Pic⁰_{C/k}`.**
192	
193	For a smooth proper geometrically integral curve `C/k`, the identity component
194	`Pic⁰_{C/k}` is smooth over `k` (Kleiman §5 Cor.~`cor:sm` + Cor.~`cor:ch0` in
195	characteristic zero + Ex.~`ex:jac` for the curve case).
196	
197	The proof (iter-194+): smoothness at the identity propagates to smoothness
198	everywhere by translation (Pic⁰ is a `k`-group scheme, so multiplication by
199	any `λ ∈ Pic⁰(k̄)` is an automorphism). At the identity, smoothness reduces
200	to the inequality
201	`dim_0 Pic⁰_{C/k} ≤ dim_k T₀ Pic⁰_{C/k} = dim_k H¹(C, 𝒪_C) = g(C)`
202	(`tangentSpaceIso`) becoming an equality. For a smooth proper curve this
203	equality holds: in characteristic zero by Cartier's theorem (every
204	`k`-group scheme is smooth), in positive characteristic by the curve-specific
205	vanishing `H²(C, 𝒪_C) = 0` (the obstruction to lifting tangent vectors). -/
206	theorem smooth {k : Type u} [Field k]
207	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
208	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
209	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
210	    Smooth (Pic0Scheme C).hom :=
211	  sorry
212	
213	/-- **Properness of `Pic⁰_{C/k}`.**
214	
215	For a smooth proper geometrically integral curve `C/k`, the identity component
216	`Pic⁰_{C/k}` is proper over `k` (Kleiman §5 Thm.~`th:qpp&p`; a smooth proper
217	curve is geometrically normal, hence the projectivity upgrade applies).
218	
219	The proof (iter-194+): `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is quasi-projective (Kleiman §5
220	Thm.~`th:qpp&p`, first conclusion); to upgrade to projective (hence proper)
221	we use the Chevalley–Rosenlicht structure theorem to reduce to ruling out
222	non-constant `k̄`-morphisms `𝔾_m → Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`. The latter follows from
223	normality of `C ×_k 𝔾_m` and Hartshorne II Ex.~6.15 (invertible sheaves on
224	a normal integral scheme are sheaves of Cartier divisors). -/
225	theorem proper {k : Type u} [Field k]
226	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
227	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
228	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
229	    IsProper (Pic0Scheme C).hom :=
230	  sorry
231	
232	/-- **Geometric irreducibility of `Pic⁰_{C/k}`.**
233	
234	For a smooth proper geometrically integral curve `C/k`, the identity component
235	`Pic⁰_{C/k}` is geometrically irreducible over `k` (Kleiman §5 Prp.~`prp:pic0`,
236	specialisation of the abstract identity-component substrate
237	`IdentityComponent.isFiniteTypeGeometricallyIrreducible` of
238	`Picard/IdentityComponent.lean` to `G = PicScheme C`).
239	
240	The proof (iter-194+): apply `GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isFiniteTypeGeometricallyIrreducible`
241	to `G = PicScheme C` (locally of finite type by `FGAPicRepresentability`), and
242	identify `IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)` with `Pic0Scheme C` via the
243	`Pic0Scheme` definition (sibling). -/
244	theorem geometricallyIrreducible {k : Type u} [Field k]
245	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
246	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
247	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
248	    GeometricallyIrreducible (Pic0Scheme C).hom :=
249	  sorry
250	
251	/-- **`Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety (A.3.vii assembly).**
252	
253	For a smooth proper geometrically integral curve `C/k`, the identity
254	component `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety over `k`: the underlying
255	`k`-scheme is smooth (`Pic0.smooth`), proper (`Pic0.proper`), and
256	geometrically irreducible (`Pic0.geometricallyIrreducible`), and it carries
257	a `k`-group-scheme structure inherited from `Pic_{C/k}` via the inclusion
258	`Pic⁰_{C/k} ↪ Pic_{C/k}`
259	(`GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isSubgroupHomomorphism`). Commutativity is
260	automatic (Milne §I.1, Cor.~1.4).
261	
262	Milne §I.1, p.~8: "A complete connected group variety is called an abelian
263	variety." This is the load-bearing A.3.vii gate of Route~A for
264	`thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness`.
265	
266	The proof (iter-194+) assembles the four conjuncts: `proper` (this file),
267	`smooth` (this file), `geometricallyIrreducible` (this file), and the
268	`Nonempty (GrpObj (Pic0Scheme C))` slot, the latter from
269	`GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isSubgroupHomomorphism` applied to
270	`G = PicScheme C` (sibling `Picard/IdentityComponent.lean`). -/
271	theorem isAbelianVariety {k : Type u} [Field k]
272	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
273	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
274	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
275	    IsProper (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧ Smooth (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧
276	      GeometricallyIrreducible (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧
277	      Nonempty (GrpObj (Pic0Scheme C)) :=
278	  sorry
279	
280	end Pic0
281	
282	end Scheme
283	
284	end AlgebraicGeometry

6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK
8	
9	/-!
10	# Genus of a smooth proper curve
11	
12	The genus of a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible curve over a field.
13	
14	## Status (closed iteration 011 — `genus`)
15	
16	The iter-011 refactor added the `noncomputable` modifier (user-authorised
17	`2026-05-07`) and an import of `AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK`
18	so the body uses the project's `ModuleCat k`-flavoured cohomology
19	`Scheme.HModule` (iter-009) of the structure sheaf
20	`Scheme.toModuleKSheaf` (iter-006). The body is the probe-confirmed
21	one-liner
22	
23	    Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)
24	
25	which is the honest mathematical definition `dim_k H^1(C, O_C)`.
26	
27	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Genus.tex` for the informal proof sketch and
28	`.archon/STRATEGY.md` for the multi-phase build-out plan.
29	-/
30	
31	set_option autoImplicit false
32	
33	universe u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
38	
39	/-- The genus of a smooth proper curve. -/
40	noncomputable def genus {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
41	    [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
42	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : ℕ :=
43	  Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)
44	
45	end AlgebraicGeometry

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Tangent-space substrate: derivations and the cotangent dual (A.3.iii substrate)
10	
11	Commutative-algebra substrate for the Kleiman §5 tangent-space theorem
12	`thm:pic0_tangent_space_iso` (`Picard/Pic0AbelianVariety.lean`,
13	`Scheme.Pic0.tangentSpaceIso`): the identification of the Zariski tangent
14	space of a `k`-scheme at a `k`-rational point with the dual of the cotangent
15	space `m/m²`, expressed at the level of the local ring at the point.
16	
17	Throughout, `R` is a local `k`-algebra. The `k`-rational-point hypothesis is
18	`Function.Bijective (algebraMap k (ResidueField R))` — the structure map to
19	the residue field is an isomorphism.
20	
21	## Main declarations
22	
23	- `AlgebraicGeometry.maximalIdeal_smul_residueField` — elements of the
24	  maximal ideal act by zero on the residue field.
25	- `AlgebraicGeometry.derivationCotangentDual` — a `k`-derivation
26	  `R → ResidueField R` kills `m²`, hence descends to an `R`-linear map
27	  `CotangentSpace R →ₗ[R] ResidueField R` on `m/m²`.
28	- `AlgebraicGeometry.derivationEquivCotangentDual` — for a `k`-rational
29	  local `k`-algebra, descent is an additive equivalence
30	  `Derivation k R (ResidueField R) ≃+ (CotangentSpace R →ₗ[R] ResidueField R)`,
31	  with inverse built from the canonical splitting `R = k ⊕ m`.
32	- `AlgebraicGeometry.localDualNumberHomEquivDerivation` — local `k`-algebra
33	  maps `R → k[ε]` are `k`-derivations `R → ResidueField R` (`ε`-coefficient).
34	- `AlgebraicGeometry.localDualNumberHomEquivCotangentSpaceDual` — the
35	  capstone: dual-number points of the closed point form the `κ`-linear dual
36	  of the cotangent space, `Module.Dual (ResidueField R) (CotangentSpace R)`.
37	
38	This is the "tangent space via dual numbers" paragraph of Kleiman §5
39	Thm. 5.11: points of a scheme over the dual numbers lifting a `k`-rational
40	point `e` correspond to local `k`-algebra maps `𝒪_e → k[ε]`, i.e. to
41	`k`-derivations `𝒪_e → k`, i.e. by this file to `Hom(m_e/m_e², k)`.
42	
43	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_Pic0AbelianVariety.tex`,
44	§ `sec:pic0_tangent_space` (`lem:tangent_derivation_cotangent_dual` and
45	friends), feeding `thm:pic0_tangent_space_iso`.
46	-/
47	
48	set_option autoImplicit false
49	
50	universe u v
51	
52	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
53	
54	open IsLocalRing
55	
56	section MaximalIdealAction
57	
58	variable {R : Type v} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
59	
60	/-- Elements of the maximal ideal act by zero on the residue field. -/
61	lemma maximalIdeal_smul_residueField {x : R} (hx : x ∈ maximalIdeal R)
62	    (c : ResidueField R) : x • c = 0 := by
63	  rw [Algebra.smul_def]
64	  have hx0 : algebraMap R (ResidueField R) x = 0 :=
65	    Ideal.Quotient.eq_zero_iff_mem.mpr hx
66	  rw [hx0, zero_mul]
67	
68	end MaximalIdealAction
69	
70	section DerivationCotangent
71	
72	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] {R : Type v} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
73	  [Algebra k R]
74	
75	/-- The restriction of a `k`-derivation `d : R → ResidueField R` to the
76	maximal ideal, as an `R`-linear map `m →ₗ[R] ResidueField R`. Linearity over
77	`R` (not merely `k`) holds because the second Leibniz term `x • d r` dies:
78	`x ∈ m` acts by zero on the residue field. -/
79	noncomputable def derivationRestrictMaximalIdeal (d : Derivation k R (ResidueField R)) :
80	    maximalIdeal R →ₗ[R] ResidueField R where
81	  toFun x := d (x : R)
82	  map_add' x y := by simp
83	  map_smul' r x := by
84	    have hrx : ((r • x : maximalIdeal R) : R) = r * (x : R) := rfl
85	    rw [hrx, Derivation.leibniz, maximalIdeal_smul_residueField x.2 (d r),
86	      add_zero, RingHom.id_apply]
87	
88	@[simp]
89	lemma derivationRestrictMaximalIdeal_apply (d : Derivation k R (ResidueField R))
90	    (x : maximalIdeal R) :
91	    derivationRestrictMaximalIdeal d x = d (x : R) := rfl
92	
93	/-- A `k`-derivation `R → ResidueField R` kills `m • m`, hence descends to the
94	cotangent space `m/m²`, `R`-linearly. -/
95	noncomputable def derivationCotangentDual (d : Derivation k R (ResidueField R)) :
96	    CotangentSpace R →ₗ[R] ResidueField R :=
97	  Submodule.liftQ _ (derivationRestrictMaximalIdeal d) (by
98	    rw [Submodule.smul_le]
99	    intro r hr x _
100	    simp only [LinearMap.mem_ker, LinearMap.map_smul,
101	      derivationRestrictMaximalIdeal_apply]
102	    exact maximalIdeal_smul_residueField hr _)
103	
104	@[simp]
105	lemma derivationCotangentDual_toCotangent (d : Derivation k R (ResidueField R))
106	    (x : maximalIdeal R) :
107	    derivationCotangentDual d ((maximalIdeal R).toCotangent x) = d (x : R) := rfl
108	
109	section Rational
110	
111	variable (hres : Function.Bijective (algebraMap k (ResidueField R)))
112	
113	/-- The `k`-rational-point hypothesis packaged as a ring equivalence
114	`k ≃+* ResidueField R`. -/
115	noncomputable def residueFieldEquivOfBijective : k ≃+* ResidueField R :=
116	  RingEquiv.ofBijective _ hres
117	
118	@[simp]
119	lemma residueFieldEquivOfBijective_apply (c : k) :
120	    residueFieldEquivOfBijective (R := R) hres c
121	      = algebraMap k (ResidueField R) c := rfl
122	
123	lemma residue_algebraMap (c : k) :
124	    residue R (algebraMap k R c) = algebraMap k (ResidueField R) c := by
125	  rw [IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply k R (ResidueField R) c]
126	  rfl
127	
128	/-- The canonical "constant part" of `r : R` at a `k`-rational point: the
129	image in `R` of the residue class of `r`, along the inverse of the residue
130	isomorphism. Subtracting it lands in the maximal ideal. -/
131	noncomputable def sectOfBijective (r : R) : R :=
132	  algebraMap k R ((residueFieldEquivOfBijective (R := R) hres).symm (residue R r))
133	
134	lemma residue_sectOfBijective (r : R) :
135	    residue R (sectOfBijective hres r) = residue R r := by
136	  unfold sectOfBijective
137	  rw [residue_algebraMap]
138	  exact (residueFieldEquivOfBijective (R := R) hres).apply_symm_apply _
139	
140	lemma sub_sectOfBijective_mem_maximalIdeal (r : R) :
141	    r - sectOfBijective hres r ∈ maximalIdeal R := by
142	  have h : residue R (r - sectOfBijective hres r) = 0 := by
143	    rw [map_sub, residue_sectOfBijective, sub_self]
144	  exact Ideal.Quotient.eq_zero_iff_mem.mp h
145	
146	lemma sectOfBijective_algebraMap (c : k) :
147	    sectOfBijective hres (algebraMap k R c) = algebraMap k R c := by
148	  unfold sectOfBijective
149	  rw [residue_algebraMap]
150	  congr 1
151	  exact (residueFieldEquivOfBijective (R := R) hres).symm_apply_apply c
152	
153	lemma sectOfBijective_of_mem {x : R} (hx : x ∈ maximalIdeal R) :
154	    sectOfBijective hres x = 0 := by
155	  have h0 : residue R x = 0 := Ideal.Quotient.eq_zero_iff_mem.mpr hx
156	  unfold sectOfBijective
157	  rw [h0, map_zero, map_zero]
158	
159	lemma sectOfBijective_add (r s : R) :
160	    sectOfBijective hres (r + s)
161	      = sectOfBijective hres r + sectOfBijective hres s := by
162	  unfold sectOfBijective
163	  rw [map_add, map_add, map_add]
164	
165	lemma sectOfBijective_mul (r s : R) :
166	    sectOfBijective hres (r * s)
167	      = sectOfBijective hres r * sectOfBijective hres s := by
168	  unfold sectOfBijective
169	  rw [map_mul, map_mul, map_mul]
170	
171	lemma sectOfBijective_one : sectOfBijective hres (1 : R) = 1 := by
172	  unfold sectOfBijective
173	  rw [map_one, map_one, map_one]
174	
175	/-- If `r - sect r ∈ m`, then `r` and its constant part act identically on the
176	residue field. -/
177	lemma smul_residueField_eq_sectOfBijective_smul (r : R) (c : ResidueField R) :
178	    r • c = sectOfBijective hres r • c := by
179	  have h : r • c - sectOfBijective hres r • c = (r - sectOfBijective hres r) • c :=
180	    (sub_smul r (sectOfBijective hres r) c).symm
181	  have h0 : (r - sectOfBijective hres r) • c = 0 :=
182	    maximalIdeal_smul_residueField (sub_sectOfBijective_mem_maximalIdeal hres r) c
183	  exact sub_eq_zero.mp (h.trans h0)
184	
185	/-- The element of the maximal ideal cut out by `r : R` under the canonical
186	splitting `R = k ⊕ m` at a `k`-rational point. -/
187	noncomputable def maximalIdealPart (r : R) : maximalIdeal R :=
188	  ⟨r - sectOfBijective hres r, sub_sectOfBijective_mem_maximalIdeal hres r⟩
189	
190	@[simp]
191	lemma maximalIdealPart_coe (r : R) :
192	    (maximalIdealPart hres r : R) = r - sectOfBijective hres r := rfl
193	
194	lemma maximalIdealPart_of_mem {x : R} (hx : x ∈ maximalIdeal R) :
195	    maximalIdealPart hres x = ⟨x, hx⟩ := by
196	  ext
197	  rw [maximalIdealPart_coe, sectOfBijective_of_mem hres hx, sub_zero]
198	
199	lemma maximalIdealPart_add (r s : R) :
200	    maximalIdealPart hres (r + s)
201	      = maximalIdealPart hres r + maximalIdealPart hres s := by
202	  ext
203	  simp only [maximalIdealPart_coe, Submodule.coe_add, sectOfBijective_add]
204	  ring
205	
206	/-- The Leibniz decomposition of the maximal-ideal part of a product. The
207	last summand lies in `m²`. -/
208	lemma maximalIdealPart_mul (r s : R) :
209	    maximalIdealPart hres (r * s)
210	      = sectOfBijective hres r • maximalIdealPart hres s
211	        + sectOfBijective hres s • maximalIdealPart hres r
212	        + ⟨(r - sectOfBijective hres r) * (s - sectOfBijective hres s),
213	           Ideal.mul_mem_right _ _ (sub_sectOfBijective_mem_maximalIdeal hres r)⟩ := by
214	  ext
215	  simp only [maximalIdealPart_coe, Submodule.coe_add, SetLike.val_smul,
216	    smul_eq_mul, sectOfBijective_mul]
217	  ring
218	
219	/-- The inverse construction: an `R`-linear functional on the cotangent space
220	`m/m²` extends to a `k`-derivation `R → ResidueField R` via the canonical
221	splitting `R = k ⊕ m` at a `k`-rational point. -/
222	noncomputable def cotangentDualToDerivation
223	    (φ : CotangentSpace R →ₗ[R] ResidueField R) :
224	    Derivation k R (ResidueField R) where
225	  toFun r := φ ((maximalIdeal R).toCotangent (maximalIdealPart hres r))
226	  map_add' r s := by
227	    rw [maximalIdealPart_add, map_add, map_add]
228	  map_smul' c r := by
229	    have hpart : maximalIdealPart hres (c • r)
230	        = algebraMap k R c • maximalIdealPart hres r := by
231	      ext
232	      simp only [maximalIdealPart_coe, SetLike.val_smul, smul_eq_mul]
233	      have hsm : (c • r : R) = algebraMap k R c * r := Algebra.smul_def c r
234	      rw [hsm, sectOfBijective_mul, sectOfBijective_algebraMap]
235	      ring
236	    rw [hpart, map_smul, map_smul, RingHom.id_apply, algebraMap_smul]
237	  map_one_eq_zero' := by
238	    change φ ((maximalIdeal R).toCotangent (maximalIdealPart hres (1 : R))) = 0
239	    have h1 : maximalIdealPart hres (1 : R) = 0 := by
240	      ext
241	      rw [maximalIdealPart_coe, sectOfBijective_one, sub_self,
242	        ZeroMemClass.coe_zero]
243	    rw [h1, map_zero, map_zero]
244	  leibniz' r s := by
245	    change φ ((maximalIdeal R).toCotangent (maximalIdealPart hres (r * s)))
246	        = r • φ ((maximalIdeal R).toCotangent (maximalIdealPart hres s))
247	          + s • φ ((maximalIdeal R).toCotangent (maximalIdealPart hres r))
248	    have hsq : (maximalIdeal R).toCotangent
249	        ⟨(r - sectOfBijective hres r) * (s - sectOfBijective hres s),
250	         Ideal.mul_mem_right _ _ (sub_sectOfBijective_mem_maximalIdeal hres r)⟩
251	          = 0 := by
252	      rw [Ideal.toCotangent_eq_zero, pow_two]
253	      exact Ideal.mul_mem_mul
254	        (sub_sectOfBijective_mem_maximalIdeal hres r)
255	        (sub_sectOfBijective_mem_maximalIdeal hres s)
256	    rw [maximalIdealPart_mul]
257	    simp only [map_add, map_smul, hsq, add_zero]
258	    rw [← smul_residueField_eq_sectOfBijective_smul hres r,
259	      ← smul_residueField_eq_sectOfBijective_smul hres s]
260	
261	@[simp]
262	lemma cotangentDualToDerivation_apply
263	    (φ : CotangentSpace R →ₗ[R] ResidueField R) (r : R) :
264	    cotangentDualToDerivation hres φ r
265	      = φ ((maximalIdeal R).toCotangent (maximalIdealPart hres r)) := rfl
266	
267	/-- **Derivations are the cotangent dual** (Kleiman §5, tangent space via dual
268	numbers, algebraic core). For a local `k`-algebra `R` whose residue field is
269	`k` (a `k`-rational point), descent to `m/m²` is an additive equivalence
270	between `k`-derivations `R → ResidueField R` and `R`-linear functionals on
271	the cotangent space. -/
272	noncomputable def derivationEquivCotangentDual :
273	    Derivation k R (ResidueField R) ≃+ (CotangentSpace R →ₗ[R] ResidueField R) where
274	  toFun d := derivationCotangentDual d
275	  invFun φ := cotangentDualToDerivation hres φ
276	  left_inv d := by
277	    ext r
278	    simp only [cotangentDualToDerivation_apply, derivationCotangentDual_toCotangent,
279	      maximalIdealPart_coe, map_sub]
280	    have : d (sectOfBijective hres r) = 0 := by
281	      unfold sectOfBijective
282	      exact d.map_algebraMap _
283	    rw [this, sub_zero]
284	  right_inv φ := by
285	    ext x
286	    obtain ⟨y, rfl⟩ := Ideal.toCotangent_surjective (maximalIdeal R) x
287	    rw [derivationCotangentDual_toCotangent, cotangentDualToDerivation_apply,
288	      maximalIdealPart_of_mem hres y.2]
289	  map_add' d₁ d₂ := by
290	    apply LinearMap.ext
291	    intro x
292	    obtain ⟨y, rfl⟩ := Ideal.toCotangent_surjective (maximalIdeal R) x
293	    simp [derivationCotangentDual_toCotangent]
294	
295	/-- `r • c = residue r * c` on the residue field. -/
296	lemma smul_residueField_def (r : R) (c : ResidueField R) :
297	    r • c = residue R r * c := by
298	  rw [Algebra.smul_def]; rfl
299	
300	lemma algebraMap_residueFieldEquiv_symm (y : ResidueField R) :
301	    algebraMap k (ResidueField R)
302	      ((residueFieldEquivOfBijective (R := R) hres).symm y) = y :=
303	  (residueFieldEquivOfBijective (R := R) hres).apply_symm_apply y
304	
305	lemma residueFieldEquiv_symm_algebraMap (c : k) :
306	    (residueFieldEquivOfBijective (R := R) hres).symm
307	      (algebraMap k (ResidueField R) c) = c :=
308	  (residueFieldEquivOfBijective (R := R) hres).symm_apply_apply c
309	
310	section DualNumbers
311	
312	open TrivSqZeroExt
313	
314	/-- A `k`-algebra map `R → k[ε]` that kills the maximal ideal in the constant
315	component (equivalently: a local homomorphism, since the maximal ideal of
316	`k[ε]` is `(ε) = ker fst`) has constant component the residue class:
317	`fst (f r)` is `residue r` read through `k ≅ ResidueField R`. -/
318	lemma fst_dualNumberHom_eq (f : R →ₐ[k] DualNumber k)
319	    (hf : ∀ x ∈ maximalIdeal R, fst (f x) = 0) (r : R) :
320	    fst (f r) = (residueFieldEquivOfBijective (R := R) hres).symm (residue R r) := by
321	  have hsplit : r = algebraMap k R
322	      ((residueFieldEquivOfBijective (R := R) hres).symm (residue R r))
323	      + (r - sectOfBijective hres r) := by
324	    unfold sectOfBijective
325	    ring
326	  calc fst (f r)
327	      = fst (f (algebraMap k R
328	            ((residueFieldEquivOfBijective (R := R) hres).symm (residue R r))))
329	        + fst (f (r - sectOfBijective hres r)) := by
330	        conv_lhs => rw [hsplit]
331	        rw [map_add, fst_add]
332	    _ = (residueFieldEquivOfBijective (R := R) hres).symm (residue R r) := by
333	        rw [hf _ (sub_sectOfBijective_mem_maximalIdeal hres r), add_zero,
334	          AlgHom.commutes]
335	        simp [TrivSqZeroExt.algebraMap_eq_inl']
336	
337	/-- The `ε`-coefficient of a local `k`-algebra map `R → k[ε]`, as a
338	`k`-derivation `R → ResidueField R`. -/
339	noncomputable def dualNumberHomToDerivation (f : R →ₐ[k] DualNumber k)
340	    (hf : ∀ x ∈ maximalIdeal R, fst (f x) = 0) :
341	    Derivation k R (ResidueField R) where
342	  toFun r := algebraMap k (ResidueField R) (snd (f r))
343	  map_add' r s := by rw [map_add, snd_add, map_add]
344	  map_smul' c r := by
345	    rw [map_smul, snd_smul, RingHom.id_apply, smul_eq_mul, map_mul,
346	      ← Algebra.smul_def]
347	  map_one_eq_zero' := by
348	    change algebraMap k (ResidueField R) (snd (f 1)) = 0
349	    rw [map_one, snd_one, map_zero]
350	  leibniz' r s := by
351	    change algebraMap k (ResidueField R) (snd (f (r * s)))
352	        = r • algebraMap k (ResidueField R) (snd (f s))
353	          + s • algebraMap k (ResidueField R) (snd (f r))
354	    rw [map_mul, snd_mul, map_add, smul_eq_mul, op_smul_eq_mul,
355	      map_mul, map_mul,
356	      fst_dualNumberHom_eq hres f hf r, fst_dualNumberHom_eq hres f hf s,
357	      algebraMap_residueFieldEquiv_symm hres, algebraMap_residueFieldEquiv_symm hres,
358	      smul_residueField_def, smul_residueField_def]
359	    ring
360	
361	@[simp]
362	lemma dualNumberHomToDerivation_apply (f : R →ₐ[k] DualNumber k)
363	    (hf : ∀ x ∈ maximalIdeal R, fst (f x) = 0) (r : R) :
364	    dualNumberHomToDerivation hres f hf r
365	      = algebraMap k (ResidueField R) (snd (f r)) := rfl
366	
367	/-- The `k`-algebra map `R → k[ε]` attached to a `k`-derivation
368	`D : R → ResidueField R`: constant component the residue class, `ε`-component
369	`D`. -/
370	noncomputable def derivationToDualNumberHom (D : Derivation k R (ResidueField R)) :
371	    R →ₐ[k] DualNumber k where
372	  toFun r := inl ((residueFieldEquivOfBijective (R := R) hres).symm (residue R r))
373	    + inr ((residueFieldEquivOfBijective (R := R) hres).symm (D r))
374	  map_one' := by
375	    rw [map_one, map_one, Derivation.map_one_eq_zero, map_zero, inr_zero, add_zero,
376	      inl_one]
377	  map_mul' r s := by
378	    have hD : (residueFieldEquivOfBijective (R := R) hres).symm (D (r * s))
379	        = (residueFieldEquivOfBijective (R := R) hres).symm (residue R r)
380	            * (residueFieldEquivOfBijective (R := R) hres).symm (D s)
381	          + (residueFieldEquivOfBijective (R := R) hres).symm (D r)
382	            * (residueFieldEquivOfBijective (R := R) hres).symm (residue R s) := by
383	      rw [Derivation.leibniz, smul_residueField_def, smul_residueField_def,
384	        map_add, map_mul, map_mul]
385	      ring
386	    rw [map_mul, map_mul, hD]
387	    rw [add_mul, mul_add, mul_add, inl_mul_inl, inl_mul_inr, inr_mul_inl,
388	      inr_mul_inr, add_zero, inr_add, smul_eq_mul, op_smul_eq_mul]
389	    abel
390	  map_zero' := by
391	    rw [map_zero, map_zero, map_zero, map_zero, inl_zero, inr_zero, add_zero]
392	  map_add' r s := by
393	    rw [map_add, map_add, map_add, map_add, inl_add, inr_add]
394	    abel
395	  commutes' c := by
396	    rw [residue_algebraMap, residueFieldEquiv_symm_algebraMap hres,
397	      Derivation.map_algebraMap, map_zero, inr_zero, add_zero,
398	      TrivSqZeroExt.algebraMap_eq_inl']
399	    simp
400	
401	@[simp]
402	lemma derivationToDualNumberHom_apply (D : Derivation k R (ResidueField R)) (r : R) :
403	    derivationToDualNumberHom hres D r
404	      = inl ((residueFieldEquivOfBijective (R := R) hres).symm (residue R r))
405	        + inr ((residueFieldEquivOfBijective (R := R) hres).symm (D r)) := rfl
406	
407	lemma derivationToDualNumberHom_fst_of_mem (D : Derivation k R (ResidueField R))
408	    {x : R} (hx : x ∈ maximalIdeal R) :
409	    fst (derivationToDualNumberHom hres D x) = 0 := by
410	  have h0 : residue R x = 0 := Ideal.Quotient.eq_zero_iff_mem.mpr hx
411	  rw [derivationToDualNumberHom_apply, fst_add, fst_inl, fst_inr, add_zero,
412	    h0, map_zero]
413	
414	/-- **Dual-number points are derivations** (Kleiman §5, tangent space via
415	dual numbers). For a local `k`-algebra `R` with residue field `k`, the local
416	`k`-algebra maps `R → k[ε]` (those killing the maximal ideal in the constant
417	component) correspond to `k`-derivations `R → ResidueField R`. -/
418	noncomputable def localDualNumberHomEquivDerivation :
419	    {f : R →ₐ[k] DualNumber k // ∀ x ∈ maximalIdeal R, fst (f x) = 0}
420	      ≃ Derivation k R (ResidueField R) where
421	  toFun f := dualNumberHomToDerivation hres f.1 f.2
422	  invFun D := ⟨derivationToDualNumberHom hres D,
423	    fun _ hx => derivationToDualNumberHom_fst_of_mem hres D hx⟩
424	  left_inv f := by
425	    apply Subtype.ext
426	    apply AlgHom.ext
427	    intro r
428	    rw [derivationToDualNumberHom_apply, dualNumberHomToDerivation_apply,
429	      residueFieldEquiv_symm_algebraMap hres,
430	      ← fst_dualNumberHom_eq hres f.1 f.2 r]
431	    exact inl_fst_add_inr_snd_eq (f.1 r)
432	  right_inv D := by
433	    ext r
434	    rw [dualNumberHomToDerivation_apply, derivationToDualNumberHom_apply,
435	      snd_add, snd_inl, snd_inr, zero_add,
436	      algebraMap_residueFieldEquiv_symm hres]
437	
438	/-- **The tangent space is the cotangent dual** (Kleiman §5 Thm. 5.11,
439	"tangent space via dual numbers", algebraic form): for a local `k`-algebra
440	`R` with residue field `k`, the set of local `k`-algebra maps `R → k[ε]`
441	(the dual-number points of `Spec R` over the closed point) is in bijection
442	with the `R`-linear functionals on the cotangent space `m/m²`. -/
443	noncomputable def localDualNumberHomEquivCotangentDual :
444	    {f : R →ₐ[k] DualNumber k // ∀ x ∈ maximalIdeal R, fst (f x) = 0}
445	      ≃ (CotangentSpace R →ₗ[R] ResidueField R) :=
446	  (localDualNumberHomEquivDerivation hres).trans
447	    (derivationEquivCotangentDual hres).toEquiv
448	
449	/-- `R`-linear functionals on the cotangent space are automatically
450	`ResidueField R`-linear: both sides are annihilated by the maximal ideal and
451	the residue map is surjective. -/
452	noncomputable def cotangentDualExtendScalars :
453	    (CotangentSpace R →ₗ[R] ResidueField R)
454	      ≃ₗ[R] Module.Dual (ResidueField R) (CotangentSpace R) :=
455	  LinearMap.extendScalarsOfSurjectiveEquiv Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
456	
457	/-- **The tangent space is the dual of the cotangent space** (Kleiman §5,
458	Thm. 5.11, first step), in its `κ`-linear form: the dual-number points of a
459	`k`-rational point of a local `k`-algebra `R` form the
460	`ResidueField R`-linear dual of `m/m²`. -/
461	noncomputable def localDualNumberHomEquivCotangentSpaceDual :
462	    {f : R →ₐ[k] DualNumber k // ∀ x ∈ maximalIdeal R, fst (f x) = 0}
463	      ≃ Module.Dual (ResidueField R) (CotangentSpace R) :=
464	  (localDualNumberHomEquivCotangentDual hres).trans
465	    (cotangentDualExtendScalars (R := R)).toEquiv
466	
467	end DualNumbers
468	
469	end Rational
470	
471	end DerivationCotangent
472	
473	end AlgebraicGeometry
474

6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Tangent-space substrate: dual-number points of a scheme at a point (A.3.iii substrate)
10	
11	Scheme-level substrate for the Kleiman §5 tangent-space theorem
12	`thm:pic0_tangent_space_iso` (`Picard/Pic0AbelianVariety.lean`,
13	`Scheme.Pic0.tangentSpaceIso`), bridging Mathlib's
14	`AlgebraicGeometry.SpecToEquivOfLocalRing` to the local-algebra substrate of
15	`Picard/TangentSpaceDualNumbers.lean`.
16	
17	## Main declarations
18	
19	- `AlgebraicGeometry.isLocalHom_dualNumber_iff` — a ring homomorphism from a
20	  local ring to the dual numbers `k[ε]` over a field is a *local*
21	  homomorphism iff its constant component kills the maximal ideal. The
22	  right-hand side is exactly the side condition
23	  `∀ x ∈ maximalIdeal R, fst (f x) = 0` used throughout
24	  `TangentSpaceDualNumbers.lean`.
25	- `AlgebraicGeometry.specToEquivOfLocalRingAt` — the fiber form of Mathlib's
26	  `SpecToEquivOfLocalRing`: for a local ring `R` and a *fixed* point `x : X`,
27	  morphisms `Spec R ⟶ X` sending the closed point to `x` correspond to local
28	  ring homomorphisms `𝒪_{X,x} ⟶ R`.
29	- `AlgebraicGeometry.specDualNumberAtEquiv` — the two combined: dual-number
30	  points of `X` at `x` correspond to ring homomorphisms `𝒪_{X,x} → k[ε]`
31	  whose constant component kills the maximal ideal.
32	
33	Combined with `localDualNumberHomEquivCotangentSpaceDual`
34	(`TangentSpaceDualNumbers.lean`), this identifies the dual-number points of
35	`X` at a `k`-rational point `x` with the dual of the cotangent space
36	`m_x/m_x²`, once the `k`-algebra structure on the stalk (from the structure
37	morphism of a `k`-scheme) is threaded through — the remaining step towards
38	`thm:pic0_tangent_space_iso`.
39	
40	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_Pic0AbelianVariety.tex`,
41	§ `sec:pic0_tangent_space` (`lem:tangent_dual_number_local_iff`,
42	`lem:tangent_spec_local_ring_at`, `cor:tangent_spec_dual_number_at`).
43	-/
44	
45	set_option autoImplicit false
46	set_option maxSynthPendingDepth 3
47	
48	universe u
49	
50	open CategoryTheory IsLocalRing
51	
52	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
53	
54	section DualNumberLocalHom
55	
56	open TrivSqZeroExt
57	
58	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
59	
60	/-- A ring homomorphism from a local ring to the dual numbers `k[ε]` over a
61	field is local iff its constant component kills the maximal ideal. The
62	right-hand side is the side condition on dual-number points used in
63	`TangentSpaceDualNumbers.lean`. -/
64	lemma isLocalHom_dualNumber_iff (f : R →+* DualNumber k) :
65	    IsLocalHom f ↔ ∀ x ∈ maximalIdeal R, fst (f x) = 0 := by
66	  constructor
67	  · intro hf x hx
68	    by_contra h0
69	    have hu : IsUnit (f x) := isUnit_iff_isUnit_fst.mpr (isUnit_iff_ne_zero.mpr h0)
70	    exact mem_nonunits_iff.mp ((mem_maximalIdeal x).mp hx) (hf.map_nonunit x hu)
71	  · intro h
72	    refine ⟨fun a ha => ?_⟩
73	    by_contra hna
74	    have hm : a ∈ maximalIdeal R := (mem_maximalIdeal a).mpr (mem_nonunits_iff.mpr hna)
75	    have h0 : fst (f a) = 0 := h a hm
76	    have := isUnit_iff_isUnit_fst.mp ha
77	    rw [h0] at this
78	    exact not_isUnit_zero this
79	
80	end DualNumberLocalHom
81	
82	section SpecAtPoint
83	
84	variable (X : Scheme.{u}) (R : CommRingCat.{u}) [IsLocalRing R]
85	
86	/-- **Fiber form of `SpecToEquivOfLocalRing`.** For a local ring `R`, a scheme
87	`X` and a fixed point `x : X`, morphisms `Spec R ⟶ X` sending the closed
88	point to `x` correspond to local ring homomorphisms `𝒪_{X,x} ⟶ R`. -/
89	noncomputable def specToEquivOfLocalRingAt (x : X) :
90	    {f : Spec R ⟶ X // f.base (closedPoint R) = x} ≃
91	      {φ : X.presheaf.stalk x ⟶ R // IsLocalHom φ.hom} where
92	  toFun f :=
93	    ⟨(X.presheaf.stalkCongr (Inseparable.of_eq f.2.symm)).hom ≫
94	        Scheme.stalkClosedPointTo f.1,
95	     haveI : IsLocalHom ((X.presheaf.stalkCongr
96	         (Inseparable.of_eq f.2.symm)).hom).hom := isLocalHom_of_isIso _
97	     CommRingCat.isLocalHom_comp _ _⟩
98	  invFun φ :=
99	    haveI := φ.2
100	    ⟨Spec.map φ.1 ≫ X.fromSpecStalk x, by
101	      rw [Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, Spec_closedPoint, Scheme.fromSpecStalk_closedPoint]⟩
102	  left_inv := by
103	    rintro ⟨f, rfl⟩
104	    refine Subtype.ext ?_
105	    dsimp only
106	    have hcongr : (X.presheaf.stalkCongr
107	        (Inseparable.of_eq (rfl : f.base (closedPoint R) = f.base (closedPoint R)).symm)).hom
108	          = 𝟙 _ := by
109	      simp [TopCat.Presheaf.stalkCongr]
110	    rw [hcongr, Category.id_comp]
111	    exact Scheme.Spec_stalkClosedPointTo_fromSpecStalk f
112	  right_inv := by
113	    rintro ⟨φ, hφ⟩
114	    haveI := hφ
115	    refine Subtype.ext ?_
116	    dsimp only
117	    refine TopCat.Presheaf.stalk_hom_ext _ fun U hxU ↦ ?_
118	    simp only [TopCat.Presheaf.stalkCongr_hom, TopCat.Presheaf.germ_stalkSpecializes_assoc,
119	      Scheme.germ_stalkClosedPointTo_Spec_fromSpecStalk]
120	
121	end SpecAtPoint
122	
123	section DualNumberPoints
124	
125	open TrivSqZeroExt
126	
127	variable {k : Type u} [Field k]
128	
129	/-- **Dual-number points of a scheme at a point, as stalk data.** For a field
130	`k`, a scheme `X` and a point `x : X`, morphisms
131	`Spec k[ε] ⟶ X` sending the closed point to `x` correspond to ring
132	homomorphisms `𝒪_{X,x} → k[ε]` whose constant component kills the maximal
133	ideal — the interface consumed by
134	`localDualNumberHomEquivCotangentSpaceDual`. -/
135	noncomputable def specDualNumberAtEquiv (X : Scheme.{u}) (x : X) :
136	    {f : Spec (CommRingCat.of (DualNumber k)) ⟶ X //
137	        f.base (closedPoint (DualNumber k)) = x} ≃
138	      {φ : X.presheaf.stalk x ⟶ CommRingCat.of (DualNumber k) //
139	        ∀ a ∈ maximalIdeal (X.presheaf.stalk x), fst (φ.hom a) = 0} :=
140	  (specToEquivOfLocalRingAt X (CommRingCat.of (DualNumber k)) x).trans
141	    (Equiv.subtypeEquivRight fun φ => isLocalHom_dualNumber_iff φ.hom)
142	
143	end DualNumberPoints
144	
145	end AlgebraicGeometry
146

4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.TangentSpaceStalkAlgebra
7	
8	/-!
9	# Tangent-space substrate: rational sections and the identity section (A.3.iii substrate)
10	
11	The last generic layer of the Kleiman §5 tangent-space computation before it
12	lands on `Pic⁰_{C/k}`: a *section* of the structure morphism of a `k`-scheme
13	hits a `k`-rational point, so the tangent-space description
14	`overDualNumberAtEquivCotangentSpaceDual` of
15	`Picard/TangentSpaceStalkAlgebra.lean` applies at it unconditionally. For a
16	`k`-group scheme (an `Over (Spec k)`-object with a `MonObj`/`GrpObj`
17	instance) the unit `η : 𝟙_ ⟶ G` is such a section, giving the tangent space
18	at the identity.
19	
20	## Main declarations
21	
22	- `AlgebraicGeometry.stalkStructureHom_comp_stalkClosedPointTo` — a section
23	  `e : Spec k ⟶ X` of the structure morphism retracts the structure
24	  homomorphism `k ⟶ 𝒪_{X,e(*)}` via the induced stalk map `𝒪_{X,e(*)} ⟶ k`.
25	- `AlgebraicGeometry.bijective_algebraMap_residueField_of_section` — **the
26	  image of a section is a `k`-rational point**: `k → κ(e(*))` is bijective.
27	  Injectivity is automatic (field source); surjectivity follows from the
28	  retraction because a field homomorphism `κ(e(*)) → k` is injective.
29	- `AlgebraicGeometry.overDualNumberSectionEquivCotangentSpaceDual` — at the
30	  image of a section, the over-`Spec k` dual-number points of `X` form the
31	  `κ(x)`-linear dual of the cotangent space `m_x/m_x²`; no rationality
32	  hypothesis left.
33	- `AlgebraicGeometry.GroupScheme.identitySection` — the identity section
34	  `e : Spec k ⟶ G` of a `k`-monoid/group scheme, the `.left` of the unit
35	  `η[G] : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec k)) ⟶ G`; `identitySection_comp` says it is a
36	  section of `G.hom`.
37	- `AlgebraicGeometry.GroupScheme.identityDualNumberEquivCotangentSpaceDual`
38	  — **the tangent space of a `k`-group scheme at the identity**: over-`Spec k`
39	  dual-number points at the identity-section point are the `κ(e)`-linear dual
40	  of `m_e/m_e²`. This is the generic LHS of Kleiman §5 Thm. 5.11 for
41	  `G = Pic⁰_{C/k}`; the wiring to `Pic0Scheme C` lives in
42	  `Picard/Pic0AbelianVariety.lean` (`Pic0.tangentSpaceCotangentDual`), and
43	  only the `H¹(C, 𝒪_C)` identification of `thm:pic0_tangent_space_iso`
44	  remains (gated on the `AJC.picrep` cone).
45	
46	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_Pic0AbelianVariety.tex`,
47	§ `sec:pic0_tangent_space` (`lem:tangent_section_retraction`,
48	`lem:tangent_section_rational`, `cor:tangent_section_cotangent_dual`,
49	`def:tangent_identity_section`, `thm:tangent_identity_cotangent_dual`).
50	-/
51	
52	set_option autoImplicit false
53	set_option maxSynthPendingDepth 3
54	
55	universe u
56	
57	open CategoryTheory IsLocalRing
58	
59	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
60	
61	section RationalSection
62	
63	variable {k : Type u} [Field k]
64	
65	/-- **A section retracts the structure homomorphism on stalks.** For a
66	scheme `X` over `Spec k` and a section `e : Spec k ⟶ X.left` of the
67	structure morphism, the stalk map of `e` at the closed point retracts the
68	structure homomorphism `k ⟶ 𝒪_{X, e(*)}`. Immediate from
69	`comp_eq_spec_iff` applied to `ψ = 𝟙 k`. -/
70	lemma stalkStructureHom_comp_stalkClosedPointTo
71	    (X : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) {e : Spec (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ X.left}
72	    (he : e ≫ X.hom = 𝟙 (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
73	    stalkStructureHom X.hom (e.base (closedPoint k)) ≫ Scheme.stalkClosedPointTo e
74	      = 𝟙 (CommRingCat.of k) :=
75	  (comp_eq_spec_iff X.hom e (𝟙 (CommRingCat.of k))).mp (by rwa [Spec.map_id])
76	
77	/-- **The image of a section is a `k`-rational point.** For a scheme `X`
78	over `Spec k`, a section `e : Spec k ⟶ X.left` of the structure morphism and
79	`x` the image of the closed point under `e`, the structure map
80	`k → κ(x)` to the residue field is bijective. Injectivity is automatic
81	(`k` is a field); for surjectivity, the retraction
82	`stalkStructureHom_comp_stalkClosedPointTo` descends to a field
83	homomorphism `φ : κ(x) → k` splitting `k → κ(x)`, and a splitting by an
84	injective map is forced to be an inverse: `a = algebraMap k κ(x) (φ a)`
85	because `φ` kills the difference. -/
86	theorem bijective_algebraMap_residueField_of_section
87	    (X : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) {e : Spec (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ X.left}
88	    (he : e ≫ X.hom = 𝟙 (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) {x : X.left}
89	    (hx : e.base (closedPoint k) = x) :
90	    Function.Bijective
91	      (algebraMap k (ResidueField (X.left.presheaf.stalk x))) := by
92	  subst hx
93	  have hretr := stalkStructureHom_comp_stalkClosedPointTo X he
94	  have hcomp : ∀ c : k,
95	      (Scheme.stalkClosedPointTo e).hom
96	        ((stalkStructureHom X.hom (e.base (closedPoint k))).hom c) = c := fun c => by
97	    simpa using DFunLike.congr_fun (congrArg CommRingCat.Hom.hom hretr) c
98	  set φ := IsLocalRing.ResidueField.lift (Scheme.stalkClosedPointTo e).hom with hφdef
99	  have hφ : ∀ c : k,
100	      φ (algebraMap k
101	        (ResidueField (X.left.presheaf.stalk (e.base (closedPoint k)))) c) = c := by
102	    intro c
103	    rw [← residue_algebraMap, hφdef, IsLocalRing.ResidueField.lift_residue_apply]
104	    exact hcomp c
105	  constructor
106	  · exact (algebraMap k
107	      (ResidueField (X.left.presheaf.stalk (e.base (closedPoint k))))).injective
108	  · intro a
109	    refine ⟨φ a, ?_⟩
110	    have h0 : φ (algebraMap k
111	        (ResidueField (X.left.presheaf.stalk (e.base (closedPoint k)))) (φ a) - a)
112	          = 0 := by
113	      rw [map_sub, hφ, sub_self]
114	    exact sub_eq_zero.mp (φ.injective (h0.trans (map_zero φ).symm))
115	
116	open TrivSqZeroExt in
117	/-- **The tangent space at the image of a section.** For a scheme `X` over
118	`Spec k` and a section `e` of the structure morphism, the over-`Spec k`
119	dual-number points of `X` at `x = e(*)` form the `κ(x)`-linear dual of the
120	cotangent space `m_x/m_x²` — `overDualNumberAtEquivCotangentSpaceDual` with
121	the rationality hypothesis discharged by
122	`bijective_algebraMap_residueField_of_section`. -/
123	noncomputable def overDualNumberSectionEquivCotangentSpaceDual
124	    (X : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) {e : Spec (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ X.left}
125	    (he : e ≫ X.hom = 𝟙 (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) {x : X.left}
126	    (hx : e.base (closedPoint k) = x) :
127	    {g : Spec (CommRingCat.of (DualNumber k)) ⟶ X.left //
128	        g ≫ X.hom = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap k (DualNumber k)))
129	          ∧ g.base (closedPoint (DualNumber k)) = x} ≃
130	      Module.Dual (ResidueField (X.left.presheaf.stalk x))
131	        (CotangentSpace (X.left.presheaf.stalk x)) :=
132	  overDualNumberAtEquivCotangentSpaceDual X x
133	    (bijective_algebraMap_residueField_of_section X he hx)
134	
135	end RationalSection
136	
137	namespace GroupScheme
138	
139	variable {k : Type u} [Field k]
140	
141	/-- **The identity section of a `k`-monoid/group scheme.** For an
142	`Over (Spec k)`-object `G` with a monoid-object structure, the underlying
143	scheme morphism `Spec k ⟶ G.left` of the unit `η[G] : 𝟙_ ⟶ G`. (For a group
144	scheme, `[GrpObj G]` supplies the `MonObj G` instance.) -/
145	noncomputable def identitySection (G : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) [MonObj G] :
146	    Spec (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ G.left :=
147	  (MonObj.one (X := G)).left
148	
149	/-- The identity section is a section of the structure morphism: the unit is
150	a morphism in `Over (Spec k)` out of the monoidal unit `Over.mk (𝟙 (Spec k))`. -/
151	lemma identitySection_comp (G : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) [MonObj G] :
152	    identitySection G ≫ G.hom = 𝟙 (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)) :=
153	  Over.w (MonObj.one (X := G))
154	
155	open TrivSqZeroExt in
156	/-- **The tangent space of a `k`-group scheme at the identity** (Kleiman §5
157	Thm. 5.11, LHS, generic form): for a `k`-monoid/group scheme `G`, the
158	over-`Spec k` dual-number points of `G` at the identity-section point
159	`e(*)` form the `κ(e(*))`-linear dual of the cotangent space
160	`m_{e(*)}/m_{e(*)}²`. No rationality hypothesis: the identity section makes
161	`e(*)` automatically `k`-rational. -/
162	noncomputable def identityDualNumberEquivCotangentSpaceDual
163	    (G : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) [MonObj G] :
164	    {g : Spec (CommRingCat.of (DualNumber k)) ⟶ G.left //
165	        g ≫ G.hom = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap k (DualNumber k)))
166	          ∧ g.base (closedPoint (DualNumber k))
167	              = (identitySection G).base (closedPoint k)} ≃
168	      Module.Dual
169	        (ResidueField (G.left.presheaf.stalk ((identitySection G).base (closedPoint k))))
170	        (CotangentSpace (G.left.presheaf.stalk ((identitySection G).base (closedPoint k)))) :=
171	  overDualNumberSectionEquivCotangentSpaceDual G (identitySection_comp G) rfl
172	
173	end GroupScheme
174	
175	end AlgebraicGeometry
176

\begin{proof}\leanok
