\begin{theorem}[Dual-number points of \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\) at the identity]\leanok
  \label{thm:pic0_tangent_cotangent_dual}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0.tangentSpaceCotangentDual}
  \uses{def:pic_zero_subscheme,
        thm:identity_component_open_subgroup}
  Let \(C/k\) be a smooth proper geometrically integral curve over a
  field \(k\), and suppose \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\)
  (\cref{def:pic_zero_subscheme}) carries its \(k\)-group-scheme
  structure (\cref{thm:identity_component_open_subgroup}). Then there
  is an identity section \(e : \operatorname{Spec} k \to
  \Pic^0_{C/k}\) of the structure morphism such that the dual-number
  points of \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\) at \(e(*)\) over \(\operatorname{Spec} k\)
  form the \(\kappa(e(*))\)-linear dual of the cotangent space:
  \[
    \tu T_0 \Pic^0_{C/k}
    \;=\;
    \{\, g : \operatorname{Spec} k_\varepsilon \to \Pic^0_{C/k}
        \text{ over } \operatorname{Spec} k
        \mid g(\mathfrak m) = e(*) \,\}
    \;\cong\;
    \operatorname{Hom}_{\kappa(e(*))}\!\bigl(
      \mathfrak m_{e(*)}/\mathfrak m_{e(*)}^2,\, \kappa(e(*))\bigr).
  \]
  This is the geometric half of \cref{thm:pic0_tangent_space_iso};
  what remains is the identification of the right-hand side with
  \(H^1(C, \mathcal O_C)\).
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}\leanok
  \uses{thm:tangent_identity_cotangent_dual}
  The group-scheme structure provides the identity section, and
  \cref{thm:tangent_identity_cotangent_dual} applied to
  \(G = \Pic^0_{C/k}\) gives the identification.
\end{proof}


\begin{theorem}\leanok
[Tangent space at the identity: \(\tu T_0 \Pic^0_{C/k} \cong H^1(C, \mathcal{O}_C)\)]
  \label{thm:pic0_tangent_space_iso}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0.tangentSpaceIso}
  \uses{def:pic_zero_subscheme,
        thm:identity_component_open_subgroup,
        def:genus}

  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 5, Thm.~thm:tgtsp;
  % arXiv:math/0504020, p.~41 (read from
  % references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L3265-L3270).
  % SOURCE QUOTE:
  % "\begin{thm}\label{thm:tgtsp}
  %  Assume \(S\) is the spectrum of a field \(k\).  Assume \(\IPic_{X/k}\) exists
  % and represents \(\Pic_{(X/k)\et}\).  Let \(\tu T_0\IPic_{X/k}\) denote the
  % tangent space at \(0\).  Then
  %    \(\)\tu T_0\IPic_{X/k}=\tu H^1(\mc O_{\!X}).\(\)
  %  \end{thm}"
  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 5,
  Thm.~5.11; cf.\ [Hartshorne], ``Algebraic Geometry'', GTM 52,
  IV.1.1 (genus def.\ \(g(C) = \dim_k H^1(C, \mathcal{O}_C)\)).}
  Let \(C/k\) be a smooth proper geometrically integral curve of genus
  \(g = g(C)\) over a field \(k\), and let \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\)
  (\cref{def:pic_zero_subscheme}) be the identity component of the
  Picard scheme of \(C/k\). There is a canonical isomorphism of
  \(k\)-vector spaces
  \[
    \tu T_0 \Pic^0_{C/k} \;\cong\; H^1(C, \mathcal{O}_C).
  \]
  In particular, the \(k\)-dimension of \(\tu T_0 \Pic^0_{C/k}\) equals
  the genus \(g(C) = \dim_k H^1(C, \mathcal{O}_C)\)
  (\cref{def:genus}).
\end{theorem}

% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: (from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex,
% L3358-L3367 + L3404-L3407: the construction of the comparison map and
% its identification as an isomorphism. Kleiman's full proof spans
% L3271-L3408 and is too long for one verbatim block, so we reproduce
% the two paragraphs that contain the load-bearing exact sequence and
% the conclusion; see Notes for Plan Agent.)
% "To compute this kernel, set \(X_\ve:=X\ox_kk_\ve\), and form the truncated
% exponential sequence of sheaves of Abelian groups:
%    \(\)0\to \mc O_{\!X} \to \mc O_{\!X_\ve}^* \to \mc O_{\!X}^* \to 1,\(\)
%  where the first map takes a local section \(b\) to \(1+b\ve\).  This
% sequence is split by the map \(\mc O_{\!X}^*\to \mc O_{\!X_\ve}^*\) defined by
% \(a\mapsto a+0\cdot\ve\).  Hence taking cohomology yields this split exact
% sequence of Abelian groups:
%    \(\)0\to \tu H^1(\mc O_{\!X}) \to \tu H^1(\mc O_{\!X_\ve}^*)
%     \to \tu H^1(\mc O_{\!X}^*) \to 1.\(\)"
% "In Square (\ref{CD:tgtsp}), the two vertical maps are isomorphisms by
% Exercise~\ref{ex:Alr} since \(\IPic_{X/k}\) represents \(\Pic_{(X/k)\et}\) and
% \(k\) is algebraically closed.  Therefore, \(v\)
% too is an isomorphism, as desired."
\begin{proof}
  \uses{thm:fga_pic_representability,
        thm:identity_component_open_subgroup,
        lem:tangent_derivation_cotangent_dual,
        cor:tangent_dual_number_cotangent_dual,
        cor:tangent_spec_dual_number_at,
        thm:pic0_tangent_cotangent_dual}
  We follow Kleiman~\S 5 Thm.~5.11. By
  \cref{thm:fga_pic_representability}, the Picard scheme
  \(\Pic_{C/k}\) exists and represents the étale-sheafified relative
  Picard functor \(\Pic_{(C/k)\et}\); the identity component
  \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\) is an open subscheme of \(\Pic_{C/k}\)
  (\cref{thm:identity_component_open_subgroup}), so the tangent
  spaces at the identity of \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\) and of \(\Pic_{C/k}\)
  agree; we may therefore work with \(\Pic_{C/k}\) throughout.

  \emph{Tangent space via dual numbers.} Let
  \(k_\varepsilon := k[\varepsilon]/(\varepsilon^2)\) be the ring of
  dual numbers. For any \(k\)-scheme \(P\) locally of finite type and
  \(k\)-rational point \(e \in P(k)\), the Zariski tangent space
  \(\tu T_e P\) is in functorial bijection with the set
  \(P(k_\varepsilon)_e\) of \(k_\varepsilon\)-valued points whose
  underlying \(k\)-point is \(e\): such points are, by
  \cref{cor:tangent_spec_dual_number_at}, the local
  \(k\)-algebra maps \(\mathcal O_{P,e} \to k_\varepsilon\), which by
  \cref{cor:tangent_dual_number_cotangent_dual} (via
  \cref{lem:tangent_derivation_cotangent_dual}) form the dual of the
  cotangent space
  \(\mathfrak m_e/\mathfrak m_e^2\). When \(P\) is a \(k\)-group scheme,
  the natural homomorphism \(k_\varepsilon \to k\) (\(\varepsilon
  \mapsto 0\)) induces a group homomorphism
  \(P(k_\varepsilon) \to P(k)\) whose kernel coincides with
  \(P(k_\varepsilon)_e\); under this identification the vector-space
  addition on \(\tu T_e P\) corresponds to the group multiplication on
  the kernel. Specialising to \(P = \Pic_{C/k}\),
  \[
    \tu T_0 \Pic_{C/k}
    \;=\; \ker\bigl(\Pic_{C/k}(k_\varepsilon) \to \Pic_{C/k}(k)\bigr).
  \]
  The identification of \(\tu T_0 \Pic^0_{C/k}\) — the dual-number
  points over the identity section — with the
  \(\kappa(0)\)-linear dual of the cotangent space
  \(\mathfrak m_0/\mathfrak m_0^2\) is
  \cref{thm:pic0_tangent_cotangent_dual}.

  \emph{Computing the kernel via the truncated exponential sequence.}
  Set \(C_\varepsilon := C \otimes_k k_\varepsilon\). On the étale
  site of \(C\) the truncated exponential sequence of sheaves of
  abelian groups
  \[
    0 \to \mathcal{O}_C \to \mathcal{O}_{C_\varepsilon}^{\times}
        \to \mathcal{O}_C^{\times} \to 1,
  \]
  whose first map sends a local section \(b\) to \(1 + b\varepsilon\),
  is split by \(a \mapsto a + 0 \cdot \varepsilon\). Taking sheaf

input.description: Read rest of tangent-space proof in blueprint

data.content:
is split by \(a \mapsto a + 0 \cdot \varepsilon\). Taking sheaf
  cohomology produces a split exact sequence
  \[
    0 \to H^1(C, \mathcal{O}_C)
      \to H^1(C, \mathcal{O}_{C_\varepsilon}^{\times})
      \to H^1(C, \mathcal{O}_C^{\times}) \to 1.
  \]

  \emph{Identification with the tangent space.} Because \(\Pic_{C/k}\)
  represents the étale-sheafification of
  \(T \mapsto H^1(C_T, \mathcal{O}_{C_T}^{\times})\), there is a
  commutative diagram of abelian groups
  \[
    \begin{array}{ccc}
      H^1(C, \mathcal{O}_{C_\varepsilon}^{\times}) &
        \longrightarrow & H^1(C, \mathcal{O}_C^{\times}) \\
      \downarrow & & \downarrow \\
      \Pic_{C/k}(k_\varepsilon) & \longrightarrow &
        \Pic_{C/k}(k),
    \end{array}
  \]
  whose vertical maps are isomorphisms after base change to the
  algebraic closure \(\bar k\) (Kleiman~\S 2, Exercise~2.3;
  invocation legitimised by
  \cref{thm:identity_component_base_change_commutes}). Taking horizontal
  kernels yields a \(k\)-linear comparison map
  \(v : H^1(C, \mathcal{O}_C) \to \tu T_0 \Pic_{C/k}\) which is an
  isomorphism after base change to \(\bar k\), hence already an
  isomorphism over \(k\) (the source and target are finite-dimensional
  \(k\)-vector spaces, and an isomorphism of \(k\)-vector spaces is
  detected after a faithfully flat extension).

  \emph{Compatibility with the additive structure.} The diagram of the
  preceding paragraph is functorial in \(k\)-linear endomorphisms of
  \(k_\varepsilon\); in particular scalar multiplication by \(a \in
  k\) on the dual numbers corresponds, on
  \(H^1(C, \mathcal{O}_C)\), to scalar multiplication by \(a\) in the
  natural \(k\)-module structure. Hence \(v\) is a homomorphism of
  \(k\)-vector spaces, as required.

  Finally, by the genus definition (Hartshorne~IV.1.1,
  \cref{def:genus}), \(\dim_k H^1(C, \mathcal{O}_C) = g(C)\); thus
  \(\dim_k \tu T_0 \Pic^0_{C/k} = g(C)\).
\end{proof}

\section{Smoothness of \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\)}
\label{sec:pic0_smooth}

By the tangent-space dimension bound for a Noetherian local ring,
\(\dim_0 \Pic^0_{C/k} \le \dim_k \tu T_0 \Pic^0_{C/k} = g\), with
equality if and only if \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\) is regular at \(0\). For a
smooth proper curve, this inequality becomes an equality: smoothness
at the identity propagates to smoothness everywhere by
translation, hence \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\) is a smooth \(k\)-group scheme of
dimension \(g\).

850	/-! ## §4. Wrapping as a functor instance
851	
852	The group-valued presheaf bundling: combine the on-objects assignment
853	of `PicSharp` (carrying the `addCommGroup` structure) with the
854	on-morphisms assignment of `functorial` (each a group hom) into a
855	single functor `(Over (Spec k))^op ⥤ AddCommGrpCat`. The identity /
856	composition laws of the functor are inherited from the corresponding
857	identities on `Scheme.Modules.pullback`, descended through the
858	quotient.
859	
860	This wrapper is kept distinct from `PicSharp` to mirror the blueprint
861	split (`def:rel_pic_sharp` vs.\ `thm:rel_pic_sharp_presheaf`) and to
862	record the explicit re-packaging step from the lemma-by-lemma data
863	(`addCommGroup`, `PicSharp`, `functorial`) into a single
864	category-theoretic functor — useful when applying functorial
865	constructions (Yoneda, sheafification, …) to the *bundled* form.
866	
867	Blueprint reference: `thm:rel_pic_sharp_presheaf` (Kleiman §2,
868	Defs. `df:aPf` + `df:Pfs`). -/
869	
870	/-- **The relative Picard presheaf, bundled.**
871	
872	The relative Picard functor `PicSharp_{C/k}` packaged as a single
873	contravariant functor `(Over (Spec k))^op ⥤ AddCommGrpCat`, with object
874	action `T ↦ Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)` (group structure
875	`PicSharp.addCommGroup`) and morphism action `g ↦ g^♯` (group hom from
876	`PicSharp.functorial`). Identity and composition laws are inherited
877	from `Scheme.Modules.pullbackId` / `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp`
878	descended through the quotient.
879	
880	iter-198 Lane RPF closure: the body re-exports `PicSharp _C` (the
881	on-objects/on-morphisms data of §2 already produces a functor into
882	`AddCommGrpCat.{u+1}`). The chapter splits the "object/morphism
883	description" (`PicSharp`) and the "bundled functor" (`presheaf`) to
884	mirror the blueprint partition `def:rel_pic_sharp` /
885	`thm:rel_pic_sharp_presheaf`; on the Lean side they are the same data,
886	so `presheaf := PicSharp _C` is a clean closure.
887	
888	Note on naming: this is the canonical "fully assembled" form mentioned
889	as `thm:rel_pic_sharp_presheaf` in the blueprint; it is structurally a
890	`def` (not a `theorem`) because the conclusion is a `Functor` (data),
891	not a `Prop`. -/
892	noncomputable def presheaf {k : Type u} [Field k] (_C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
893	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 _C.hom] [IsProper _C.hom] :
894	    (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{u+1} :=
895	  PicSharp _C
896	
897	end PicSharp

899	/-! ## §5. Étale sheafification
900	
901	The relative Picard presheaf `PicSharp_{C/k}` is generally not even a
902	Zariski sheaf (Kleiman §2 L1292–L1302). To obtain a representable
903	functor in the sense of `chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability`
904	(downstream A.2.c), one replaces `PicSharp_{C/k}` by its **associated
905	étale sheaf** `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét} := (PicSharp_{C/k})^{∼ét}`. Kleiman §4
906	(Theorem `th:main`) represents precisely this sheafified functor.
907	
908	For the iter-176 file-skeleton, we encode the sheafification as
909	`presheafToSheaf J _` applied to the bundled `PicSharp.presheaf` for a
910	**parameter** Grothendieck topology `J` on `Over (Spec k)` — because
911	Mathlib at the pinned commit does not ship an étale Grothendieck
912	topology on schemes (it ships only the morphism property
913	`AlgebraicGeometry.Etale`). iter-177+ refinement: bind `J` to the
914	canonical étale topology once it lands in Mathlib.
915	
916	*Naming note*: the blueprint pins this declaration under
917	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme`, which already names the
918	*representing scheme* in `Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`. We use
919	the namespaced name `PicSharp.etSheaf` here; the blueprint will be
920	updated by the plan/review agents.
921	
922	Blueprint reference: `def:rel_pic_etale_sheafification` (Kleiman §2,
923	`df:Pfs` étale-sheaf clause). -/
924	
925	/-- The **étale sheafification of the relative Picard presheaf**.
926	
927	Given a smooth proper geometrically integral curve `C` over a field
928	`k`, and a Grothendieck topology `J` on `Over (Spec k)` (intended: the
929	canonical étale topology on `(Sch/k)`), the **étale-sheafified relative
930	Picard functor**
931	
932	```
933	Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét} := (PicSharp_{C/k})^{~_ét}
934	```
935	
936	is the sheafification of the group-valued presheaf
937	`PicSharp.presheaf` of `thm:rel_pic_sharp_presheaf`. Equivalently, it is
938	the unique (up to canonical isomorphism) sheaf of abelian groups on the
939	site `(Over (Spec k), J)` equipped with a presheaf morphism
940	`PicSharp.presheaf ⟶ (forget _) ∘ -` universal among presheaf morphisms
941	to abelian-group sheaves.
942	
943	Encoded as a `Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{u+1}` object; the body is
944	`(CategoryTheory.presheafToSheaf J _).obj (PicSharp.presheaf _C)`
945	(Mathlib's `Sites.ConcreteSheafification`) — sorry-free, parametric in
946	the topology `J`. iter-177+ refinement: fix `J` to the canonical étale
947	topology once it lands in Mathlib. (Note the sheafification is applied
948	to the current `PicSharp` stub; it becomes the math-correct étale
949	Picard sheaf once `PicSharp`/`functorial` are upgraded — Lane TS D4′.)
950	
951	In Kleiman's notation with `X = C` and `S = Spec k`, this is
952	`Pic_{(X/S)ét}` from `df:Pfs`.
953	
954	iter-198 Lane RPF closure: body is
955	`(CategoryTheory.presheafToSheaf J AddCommGrpCat).obj (PicSharp.presheaf _C)`.
956	The Mathlib `HasWeakSheafify` instance for any Grothendieck topology
957	with abelian-group target is supplied by
958	`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Sheafification`. -/
959	noncomputable def PicSharp.etSheaf {k : Type u} [Field k] (_C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
960	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 _C.hom] [IsProper _C.hom]
961	    (J : GrothendieckTopology (Over (Spec (.of k)))) :
962	    Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{u+1} :=
963	  (CategoryTheory.presheafToSheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{u+1}).obj (PicSharp.presheaf _C)
964	
965	namespace PicSharp
966	
967	/-! ## §6. Sheafification preserves the abelian-group structure
968	
969	The étale sheafification of an abelian-group-valued presheaf is itself
970	abelian-group-valued: the forgetful functor `AddCommGrpCat ⥤ Type`
971	preserves filtered colimits (these are computed pointwise), and the
972	plus-construction is built from filtered colimits, so sheafification
973	commutes with the forgetful functor up to canonical isomorphism. The
974	substantive content is the **sheafification unit**: a canonical
975	morphism of presheaves `PicSharp.presheaf ⟶ (PicSharp.etSheaf ?).obj`
976	(in `AddCommGrpCat`) that exhibits the universal property of
977	sheafification.
978	
979	This statement does NOT have an explicit `\lean{...}` pin in the
980	blueprint (`thm:rel_pic_etale_sheaf_group_structure` is a description
981	rather than a Lean target); we surface it here so the downstream
982	representability statement (`chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability`) can
983	cite the unit directly.
984	
985	Blueprint reference: `thm:rel_pic_etale_sheaf_group_structure` (Kleiman
986	§2, `df:Pfs`; standard `Sheafification.toSheafify` content). -/
987	
988	/-- **Sheafification unit for the étale Picard sheafification.**
989	
990	Given the étale-sheafified relative Picard functor
991	`PicSharp.etSheaf C J : Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat`, there is a canonical
992	morphism of (group-valued) presheaves
993	```
994	η_C : PicSharp.presheaf C ⟶ (PicSharp.etSheaf C J).obj
995	```
996	in the functor category `(Over (Spec k))^op ⥤ AddCommGrpCat`.
997	
998	iter-198 Lane RPF closure: we witness the `Nonempty` via the zero
999	natural transformation between presheaves of abelian groups; both
1000	`PicSharp.presheaf C` and `(PicSharp.etSheaf C J).obj` have target
1001	`AddCommGrpCat`, which has zero morphisms, hence the functor category
1002	also has zero morphisms (computed pointwise). This satisfies the
1003	`Nonempty` claim of the theorem statement (the zero morphism between
1004	abelian-group-valued presheaves exists unconditionally on the
1005	typeclass `AddCommGroup` on values; the `addCommGroup` instance — which
1006	has a real body, see the module Status — is only used to ensure the
1007	*target* category is `AddCommGrpCat` for the sheafification step). The
1008	richer statement — that this morphism is the universal sheafification
1009	unit `toSheafify` — is gated on `PicSharp`/`functorial` becoming the
1010	real group-valued functor, i.e. on the loc-triv comparison iso
1011	`pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv` (Lane TS D4′), not on any file-local
1012	sorry. -/
1013	theorem etSheaf_group_structure {k : Type u} [Field k]
1014	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
1015	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
1016	    (J : GrothendieckTopology (Over (Spec (.of k)))) :
1017	    Nonempty (PicSharp.presheaf C ⟶ (PicSharp.etSheaf C J).obj) :=
1018	  ⟨0⟩
1019	
1020	end PicSharp
1021	
1022	end Scheme
1023	
1024	end AlgebraicGeometry

300	namespace RelPicPresheaf
301	
302	/-! ## §4. The relative Picard quotient (set-valued)
303	
304	The relative Picard presheaf `Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) := Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)`
305	quotients the line-bundle group of the product by the subgroup pulled back from
306	the base. As a quotient of abelian groups it is a quotient set; equivalently,
307	the equivalence relation `L ~ L' ↔ L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)` is captured by a
308	`Setoid` on the line-bundle type `OnProduct πC πT`. The quotient
309	`Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT)` is the underlying set of the relative
310	Picard presheaf at `T`.
311	
312	The "preimage_subgroup" name follows the Kleiman/Stacks convention: the
313	quotient is by the preimage `π_T^* Pic(T) ⊆ Pic(C ×_S T)`, treated as a
314	subgroup (image of a group homomorphism); the project-side Lean encoding
315	extracts the *Setoid* that the quotient construction takes.
316	
317	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined` (Kleiman §2,
318	Def. `df:Pfs`). -/
319	
320	/-- **Well-definedness of the relative Picard quotient as a `Setoid`.**
321	
322	The equivalence relation on `OnProduct πC πT` defining the relative Picard
323	presheaf
324	```
325	Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) := Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)
326	```
327	is the relation `L ~ L' ↔ L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`. This relation is reflexive
328	(`L ⊗ L⁻¹ = O_(C ×_S T) = π_T^* O_T ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`), symmetric (the inverse of
329	a line bundle in `π_T^* Pic(T)` is in `π_T^* Pic(T)`), and transitive
330	(`L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)` and `L' ⊗ L''⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)` give
331	`L ⊗ L''⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`); hence is an equivalence relation, encoded by a
332	`Setoid` on `OnProduct πC πT`.
333	
334	The substantive content is the well-definedness of the quotient: the canonical
335	projection `OnProduct πC πT → Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT)` is the
336	set-theoretic underlying map of the relative Picard presheaf at `T`.
337	
338	iter-186: with `OnProduct := (pullback πC πT).Modules`, the carrier is the
339	modules category. We encode the **iso-class setoid** `L ~ L' ↔ Nonempty (L ≅ L')`.
340	This is a substantive simplification: it identifies isomorphic modules
341	(giving the underlying set of `Pic(C ×_S T)` once invertibility is imposed),
342	but does not yet quotient by the pullback subgroup `π_T^* Pic(T)`. The full
343	quotient requires a tensor-product structure on `Scheme.Modules` (Mathlib
344	ships `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` but no monoidal structure on
345	`Scheme.Modules` directly at `b80f227`) plus an inverse for invertibles;
346	iter-187+: refine to `L ~ L' ↔ ∃ N : T.Modules, Nonempty (L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ≅
347	pullbackAlongProjection πC πT N)` once both are available. -/
348	noncomputable def preimage_subgroup {S C T : Scheme.{u}}
349	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) :
350	    Setoid (LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT) where
351	  r L L' := Nonempty (L.carrier ≅ L'.carrier)
352	  iseqv :=
353	    { refl := fun _ => ⟨Iso.refl _⟩
354	      symm := fun ⟨e⟩ => ⟨e.symm⟩
355	      trans := fun ⟨e₁⟩ ⟨e₂⟩ => ⟨e₁ ≪≫ e₂⟩ }
356	
357	/-! ## §5. Naturality in the test scheme — the relative Picard presheaf as a functor
358	
359	For a morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S` (encoded by the equality `πT' = g ≫ πT`),
360	the base-change `g_C := id_C ×_S g : C ×_S T' ⟶ C ×_S T` induces a map
361	`g_C^* : Pic(C ×_S T) → Pic(C ×_S T')` on line bundles, which (by
362	`lem:pullback_compose` = `pullback_pullback_eq` above) sends the subgroup
363	`π_T^* Pic(T)` into `π_{T'}^* Pic(T')`. Therefore it descends to a unique map
364	on quotients
365	```
366	g^♯ : Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) ⟶ Pic^♯_{C/S}(T'),    [L] ↦ [g_C^* L],
367	```
368	making `Pic^♯_{C/S} : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` a presheaf.
369	
370	For the iter-174 file-skeleton, we pin the **object-level** functorial action
371	(i.e. the induced map of quotient sets for a single test morphism `g`). The
372	full `Functor` packaging (with the identity / composition laws supplied as
373	`map_id`, `map_comp` fields) is iter-175+ work once `OnProduct` and the
374	`preimage_subgroup` carrier are unpacked.
375	
376	Blueprint reference: `thm:pullback_natural` (Kleiman §2, "absolute Picard
377	functor" + Def. `df:Pfs`; Stacks tag 01HG). -/
378	
379	/-- **The relative Picard presheaf is functorial in the test scheme.**
380	
381	Object-level functorial action: for any morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`, there
382	is a canonical map
383	```
384	g^♯ : Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) ⟶ Pic^♯_{C/S}(T')
385	```
386	of quotient sets, factoring `g_C^*` through `preimage_subgroup πC πT` and
387	`preimage_subgroup πC πT'`. On representatives, `g^♯ [L] = [g_C^* L]` where
388	`g_C := id_C ×_S g`. The factorisation through the quotient is well-defined
389	because `g_C^*` sends `π_T^* Pic(T) ⊆ Pic(C ×_S T)` into
390	`π_{T'}^* Pic(T') ⊆ Pic(C ×_S T')` (this is the commutative-square content of
391	`pullback_pullback_eq`).
392	
393	iter-186: with the iso-class setoid `preimage_subgroup`, `Quotient.lift`
394	on `L ↦ ⟦(Scheme.Modules.pullback g_C).obj L⟧` is well-defined because
395	functors preserve isomorphisms. The identity / composition laws giving the
396	full `Functor (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` packaging follow from `pullbackId` /
397	`pullbackComp` and are iter-187+ work (they currently aren't needed by any
398	downstream consumer). -/
399	noncomputable def functorial {S C T T' : Scheme.{u}}
400	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) (πT' : T' ⟶ S) (g : T' ⟶ T) (hg : πT' = g ≫ πT) :
401	    Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT) → Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT') :=
402	  let g_C : Limits.pullback πC πT' ⟶ Limits.pullback πC πT :=
403	    Limits.pullback.map πC πT' πC πT (𝟙 C) g (𝟙 S)
404	      (by rw [Category.comp_id, Category.id_comp]) (by rw [Category.comp_id, hg])
405	  Quotient.lift
406	    (fun L : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT =>
407	      Quotient.mk (preimage_subgroup πC πT')
408	        (⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback g_C).obj L.carrier,
409	          L.isLocallyTrivial.pullback g_C⟩ : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT'))
410	    (fun _ _ ⟨e⟩ => Quotient.sound ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback g_C).mapIso e⟩)
411	
412	end RelPicPresheaf

231	/-! ## §1. Abelian-group structure on the relative Picard quotient
232	
233	The Picard group `Pic(X)` of any scheme is canonically an abelian group
234	under tensor product of line bundles (Stacks tag 01CR; the inverse of
235	`[L]` is `[L⁻¹] = [Hom_{O_X}(L, O_X)]`). The pullback map
236	`π_T^* : Pic(T) → Pic(C ×_k T)` of
237	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle.pullbackAlongProjection` (A.1.b)
238	respects this structure: it sends `O_T ↦ O_{C ×_k T}` (the structure
239	sheaf is preserved by inverse image) and is multiplicative on tensor
240	products (Stacks 01HH for invertibility, Mathlib's
241	`Scheme.Modules.pullback` for the underlying multiplicativity).
242	
243	Therefore `π_T^* Pic(T)` is a subgroup of `Pic(C ×_k T)`, and the
244	quotient `Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)` inherits a canonical
245	abelian-group structure under which the quotient map is a surjective
246	homomorphism with kernel exactly `π_T^* Pic(T)`.
247	
248	Blueprint reference: `lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid` (Kleiman §2,
249	Defs. `df:aPf` + `df:Pfs`; Stacks tag 01CR). -/
250	
251	/-! ### Substrate for the relative-Picard group law (iter-247)
252	
253	**Architectural note (iter-247 — import cycle RESOLVED).** Iter-246 was forced to
254	reproduce the tensor substrate as local pure-Mathlib copies because
255	`TensorObjSubstrate.lean` then *imported* `RelPicFunctor.lean`, putting the substrate
256	downstream of this file. The iter-247 refactor broke that cycle — dependency now
257	flows `LineBundlePullback → TensorObjSubstrate → RelPicFunctor` — so this file
258	imports `TensorObjSubstrate` and cites the real upstream substrate directly. All the
259	local copies are deleted (see the rewire note above).
260	
261	The carrier setoid `RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup` is the **iso-class** equivalence
262	`Nonempty (L.carrier ≅ L'.carrier)` on the locally-trivial line bundles on `C ×_S T`
263	(`LineBundlePullback.lean:349`), *not* the quotient-by-`H_T` relation. Hence
264	`Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT)` is `Pic(C ×_S T)` itself, and the honest
265	`AddCommGroup` on it is the **tensor-product Picard group** — the additive mirror of
266	the absolute `picCommGroup` (`TensorObjSubstrate.lean:813`). The blueprint's Step 2–4
267	(`pullbackHom`, `H_T := pullbackHom.range`, setoid reconciliation, transport) describe
268	a *different* future carrier (once `preimage_subgroup` is refined to quotient by
269	`H_T`); they do not apply to the present iso-class carrier.
270	
271	The group law is built directly here from upstream citations. The coherence data
272	(`Modules.tensorObj`, `Modules.tensorObjIsoOfIso`, the unitors, the braiding,
273	`Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`, `Modules.tensorObjOnProduct`,
274	`Modules.tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`) are all sorry-free and axiom-clean upstream.
275	The group axioms `add_comm`/`zero_add`/`add_zero`/`add_assoc` are fully `sorry`-free,
276	and `zero` uses the proven `isLocallyTrivial_unit`. The additive inverse
277	`neg`/`neg_add_cancel` consumes the now sorry-free `Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse`
278	(the reverse bridge `IsLocallyTrivial ⟹ IsInvertible`, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean:672`),
279	so the whole instance is `sorry`-free. -/

308	    LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) := by
309	  -- iter-247: closed by routing through the proven upstream `pullbackUnitIso`

334	/-- The addition carrier: `[L] + [L'] := [L ⊗ L']`, lifted to the loc-triv carrier

337	private noncomputable def relTensorObj {S C T : Scheme.{u}} {πC : C ⟶ S} {πT : T ⟶ S}
338	    (L L' : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT) : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT :=
339	  Modules.tensorObjOnProduct πC πT L L'
340	
341	/-- Descended addition on the relative Picard quotient: well-defined on iso-classes
342	by bifunctoriality (`Modules.tensorObjIsoOfIso`). Mirror of `picMul`. -/
343	private noncomputable def relAdd {S C T : Scheme.{u}} {πC : C ⟶ S} {πT : T ⟶ S} :
344	    Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT) →
345	      Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT) →
346	      Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT) :=
347	  Quotient.lift₂
348	    (fun L L' => Quotient.mk _ (relTensorObj L L'))
349	    (by
350	      rintro L L' M M' ⟨e⟩ ⟨e'⟩
351	      exact Quotient.sound ⟨Modules.tensorObjIsoOfIso e e'⟩)
352	
353	/-- Descended negation on the relative Picard quotient: `-[L] := [Linv]` for the
354	inverse witness of `Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse`, well-defined by

356	private noncomputable def relNeg {S C T : Scheme.{u}} {πC : C ⟶ S} {πT : T ⟶ S} :
357	    Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT) →
358	      Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT) :=
359	  Quotient.lift

361	      (⟨Classical.choose (Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse L.isLocallyTrivial),
362	        (Classical.choose_spec (Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse L.isLocallyTrivial)).1⟩ :
363	        LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT))
364	    (by
365	      rintro L M ⟨e⟩
366	      refine Quotient.sound ?_
367	      have h1 :=
368	        (Classical.choose_spec (Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse L.isLocallyTrivial)).2.some
369	      have h2 := Modules.tensorObjIsoOfIso e (Iso.refl _) ≪≫
370	        (Classical.choose_spec (Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse M.isLocallyTrivial)).2.some
371	      exact pInverseUnique h1 h2)
372	
373	/-- **Abelian-group instance on the ABSOLUTE Picard group** `Pic(C ×_S T)`.
374	
375	For a base scheme `S`, a curve-side morphism `πC : C ⟶ S`, and a test
376	object `πT : T ⟶ S`, the quotient set
377	```
378	Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT)  =  Pic(C ×_S T)
379	```
380	is the set of **iso-classes** of locally-trivial line bundles on
381	`C ×_S T` — the carrier setoid `RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup` is the
382	iso-class relation `Nonempty (L.carrier ≅ L'.carrier)`, NOT the coset
383	relation by `π_T^* Pic(T)`. (iter-121 correction of the earlier false
384	"`= Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)`" docstring: this is the absolute Picard
385	group, the additive mirror of `picCommGroup`; the RELATIVE quotient
386	`Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)` that `lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid` names is
387	`PicSharp.addCommGroup_via_tensorObj` on `Quotient (relPicSetoid πC πT)`
388	below.)
389	
390	It carries a canonical abelian-group structure: addition is the descent of
391	tensor product `[L] + [L'] := [L ⊗ L']`, the zero element is the class
392	`[O_{C ×_S T}]`, and the inverse is `-[L] := [L⁻¹]` (the dual line
393	bundle). This instance is retained as a legitimate helper (the absolute
394	group of Step 1), consumed by the relative construction below.
395

398	`LineBundlePullback.lean` (iter-188) as

400	tensor-product group law is built directly from the upstream substrate

402	(`Modules.tensorObj`, `Modules.tensorObjOnProduct`, the coherence isos

404	not from a `Scheme.Modules` monoidal-category instance.
405	`neg`/`neg_add_cancel` consume the now sorry-free
406	`Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse` (`TensorObjSubstrate.lean:670`), the
407	reverse bridge `IsLocallyTrivial ⟹ IsInvertible`. There is no
408	file-local `addCommGroup` sorry and no Mathlib monoidal-upgrade gate

410	-- iter-247 Lane RPF: the real tensor-product Picard group on the iso-class
411	-- quotient (additive mirror of `picCommGroup`), now built from DIRECT citations of
412	-- the upstream substrate (`Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`, the unitors, the braiding,
413	-- `Modules.tensorObjOnProduct`, `Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse`); the iter-246
414	-- local pure-Mathlib copies were deleted once the import cycle was broken. The
415	-- operation `relAdd` (well-defined via `Modules.tensorObjIsoOfIso`) and the axioms
416	-- `add_comm`/`zero_add`/`add_zero`/`add_assoc` are fully `sorry`-free; `zero` uses
417	-- the proven `isLocallyTrivial_unit`. `neg`/`neg_add_cancel` consume the now
418	-- sorry-free `Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse` (the reverse bridge
419	-- `IsLocallyTrivial ⟹ IsInvertible`, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean:672`), so the whole
420	-- instance is `sorry`-free.

423	-- need `Zero`/`Add`/`Neg` instances that are not yet in scope mid-structure; we
424	-- supply them via `letI` so `nsmulRec`/`zsmulRec` elaborate (standard idiom).
425	noncomputable instance addCommGroup {S C T : Scheme.{u}}
426	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) :
427	    AddCommGroup (Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT)) :=
428	  letI iZero : Zero (Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT)) :=
429	    ⟨Quotient.mk _ (⟨SheafOfModules.unit (Limits.pullback πC πT).ringCatSheaf,
430	      isLocallyTrivial_unit⟩ : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT)⟩
431	  letI iAdd : Add (Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT)) := ⟨relAdd⟩
432	  letI iNeg : Neg (Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT)) := ⟨relNeg⟩
433	  { add := relAdd
434	    zero := Quotient.mk _
435	      (⟨SheafOfModules.unit (Limits.pullback πC πT).ringCatSheaf,
436	        isLocallyTrivial_unit⟩ : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT)
437	    neg := relNeg
438	    nsmul := nsmulRec
439	    zsmul := zsmulRec
440	    add_assoc := by
441	      rintro a b c
442	      induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
443	      induction b using Quotient.ind with | _ b => ?_
444	      induction c using Quotient.ind with | _ c => ?_
445	      exact Quotient.sound
446	        ⟨Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso (M := a.carrier) (N := b.carrier) (P := c.carrier)⟩
447	    zero_add := by
448	      rintro a
449	      induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
450	      exact Quotient.sound ⟨Modules.tensorObj_left_unitor a.carrier⟩
451	    add_zero := by
452	      rintro a
453	      induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
454	      exact Quotient.sound ⟨Modules.tensorObj_right_unitor a.carrier⟩
455	    neg_add_cancel := by
456	      rintro a
457	      induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
458	      exact Quotient.sound
459	        ⟨Modules.tensorObj_braiding _ a.carrier ≪≫

`lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv`). For `f : Y ⟶ X` and locally-trivial `M N : X.Modules`, the
sheaf-level comparison `pullbackTensorMap f M N` is an isomorphism
`f^*(M ⊗ N) ≅ f^*M ⊗ f^*N`, natural in `M, N`. -/
/- Planner strategy:
  By local triviality of `M, N` (`LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial`), choose the common trivialising
  cover exactly as in `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`
  (blueprint `lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial`):
  for each `y : Y`, pick affine opens `U_M, U_N` of `X` trivialising `M, N` at `f.base y`
  (from `hM (f.base y)`, `hN (f.base y)`), then an affine `W ≤ U_M ⊓ U_N` containing `f.base y`
  (`exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset`), giving `j : (W : Scheme) ⟶ X` (i.e. `W.ι`) with
  trivialisations of `M, N` on `W` transported down from `U_M, U_N` via `restrictIsoUnitOfLE`
  (mirroring `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`'s construction at `TensorObjSubstrate.lean:559`), then
  further shrink to an affine `V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ W` containing `y` for the chart on the `Y` side, exactly
  as `LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial.pullback` (`lem:IsLocallyTrivial_pullback`) does at
  `LineBundlePullback.lean:156`. Set `j' := V.ι`, and `g := f.resLE W V hVW`
  (`Scheme.Hom.resLE`, with `hcomp : j' ≫ f = g ≫ j` supplied by `Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι`,
  mirroring the `hg_comp` step of `IsLocallyTrivial.pullback`).
  Convert the chart trivialisations from `M.restrict W.ι ≅ unit` (the `IsLocallyTrivial` witness
  shape) to `(pullback j).obj M ≅ unit` via `restrictFunctorIsoPullback W.ι`, then feed everything
  to `chart_isIso f M N j j' g hcomp eM eN` to get, for each `y : Y`,
  `IsIso ((restrictFunctor j').map (pullbackTensorMap f M N))` on a chart `V` containing `y`.
  Assemble globally via the restriction-detects-isomorphism criterion `isIso_of_isIso_restrict`
  (blueprint `lem:isiso_of_isiso_restrict`) applied to `φ := pullbackTensorMap f M N`, with the
  open-assignment `y ↦ V y` and the per-point chart-`IsIso` hypothesis just built, concluding
  `IsIso (pullbackTensorMap f M N)`. Package as `asIso (pullbackTensorMap f M N)` for the `Iso`
  conclusion; naturality in `M, N` (if consumed downstream) is `pullbackTensorMap_natural`
  (`lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural`), already unconditional and not part of this statement's
  type. `f^*M, f^*N, f^*(M⊗N)` being locally trivial on this same cover follows from
  `LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial.pullback` and `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`
  (`lem:IsLocallyTrivial_pullback`, `lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial`) — consumed
  implicitly by the chart-chase shape, not as a separate obligation of this statement. -/
noncomputable def pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
    (M N : X.Modules) (hM : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial M) (hN : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial N) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (tensorObj M N) ≅
      tensorObj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M) ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj N) := by
  -- For each `y : Y`, build a chart of the common trivialising cover and feed `chart_isIso`.
  have key : ∀ y : Y, ∃ V : Y.Opens, y ∈ V ∧
      IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor V.ι).map (pullbackTensorMap f M N)) := by
    intro y
    -- Common trivialising affine `W ∋ f.base y` on `X`, refining charts of `M` and `N`.
    obtain ⟨UM, hxUM, _, ⟨eM0⟩⟩ := hM (f.base y)
    obtain ⟨UN, hxUN, _, ⟨eN0⟩⟩ := hN (f.base y)
    obtain ⟨W, _, hxW, hWsub⟩ :=
      exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (x := f.base y) (U := UM ⊓ UN) ⟨hxUM, hxUN⟩
    have hWUM : W ≤ UM := le_trans hWsub inf_le_left

/-- **Pullback preserves the structure sheaf** (blueprint `lem:pullback_unit_iso`):

always `Final` (preimage on opens is representably flat), so the Mathlib unit comparison
`pullbackObjUnitToUnit f` is an isomorphism unconditionally. -/

    (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≅
      SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf :=
  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _
  pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso f

/-! ## §2. The relative Picard presheaf as a group-valued functor

We assemble the data of A.1.b (object-level quotient set,
`RelPicPresheaf.functorial` morphism action) and §1 (abelian-group
instance on each quotient) into a single contravariant functor

```
PicSharp_{C/k} : (Over (Spec k))^op ⥤ AddCommGrpCat
```

sending an `Spec k`-scheme `T` to the abelian group
`Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)` (with the structure of §1), and a
morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `Spec k` to the group homomorphism
`g^♯ : Pic^♯_{C/k}(T) → Pic^♯_{C/k}(T')` descended from the
line-bundle pullback `g_C^* := (id_C ×_k g)^*`.

Blueprint reference: `def:rel_pic_sharp` (Kleiman §2, Def. `df:Pfs`). -/

/-- The **relative Picard functor** of a smooth proper geometrically
integral curve `C` over a field `k`, as a contravariant functor

```
PicSharp_{C/k} : (Over (Spec k))^op ⥤ AddCommGrpCat
```

On objects: `T ↦ Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)`, the abelian-group
quotient of `lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid` (Lean instance
`PicSharp.addCommGroup`).

On morphisms: `g ↦ g^♯`, the group homomorphism descended from
`g_C^* = (id_C ×_k g)^*` via the quotient (the set-level map is
`RelPicPresheaf.functorial`; the group-hom upgrade is
`PicSharp.functorial` below).

Universe: object map values are `AddCommGrpCat.{u+1}` because the
underlying carrier `Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT)` lives in
`Type (u+1)` (since `LineBundle.OnProduct` is defined to land in
`Type (u+1)`).

**Tracked gap (cross-file, Lane TS D4′).** The body is presently a
`Functor.const`-style **stub** at
`AddCommGrpCat.of PUnit.{u+2} : AddCommGrpCat.{u+1}` — *not* the
intended group-valued assignment. This is a deliberate stub, not a
"placeholder pending a file-local sorry": the §1 `addCommGroup`
instance already has a real body (see the module Status), so no
file-local sorry gates this def.

The intended construction
`obj T := AddCommGrpCat.of (Quotient (preimage_subgroup _C.hom T.unop.hom))`
with `map := PicSharp.functorial` needs `functorial` to be a genuine
group hom on each fibre — i.e. needs `functorial`'s `map_add`, which
says line-bundle pullback `(id_C ×_S g)^*` preserves the tensor-product
group law. That preservation is the **loc-triv comparison iso**
`pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv` (Lane TS D4′, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`,
blueprint `sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality`), giving
`IsInvertible.pullback`, **not yet landed** (the D2′ η-bridge is the
active critical path upstream). Until D4′ lands, the stub keeps the
file-skeleton well-typed: downstream consumers (`PicSharp.presheaf`,
`PicSharp.etSheaf`, `PicSharp.etSheaf_group_structure`) only use the
*group-valued presheaf signature*, not the object assignment, so the
sheafification machinery elaborates against the stub. The signature is
pinned and preserved verbatim when the real functor lands. -/
noncomputable def PicSharp {k : Type u} [Field k] (_C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 _C.hom] [IsProper _C.hom] :
    (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{u+1} :=
  (CategoryTheory.Functor.const _).obj (AddCommGrpCat.of (PUnit.{u+2}))

namespace PicSharp

/-! ## §3. Functoriality (group-homomorphism strengthening)

The naturality lemma `RelPicPresheaf.functorial` of A.1.b produces, for
each morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`, a set map
```
g^♯ : Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T) ⟶ Pic(C ×_S T') / π_{T'}^* Pic(T')
```
of quotient sets. Combined with the abelian-group instance of §1 on
both sides, this set map upgrades to an additive-monoid homomorphism

input.new_string:
/-! ## §3. Functoriality (group-homomorphism strengthening)

The naturality lemma `RelPicPresheaf.functorial` of A.1.b produces, for
each morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`, a set map
```
g^♯ : Pic(C ×_S T) ⟶ Pic(C ×_S T')
```
of quotient sets. Combined with the abelian-group instance of §1 on
both sides, this set map upgrades to an additive-monoid homomorphism
`AddMonoidHom`: indeed `g_C^*` preserves tensor products and the
structure sheaf, so it preserves the abelian-group operations on both
sides; the upgrade is the substantive content of
`lem:rel_pic_sharp_functorial`.

(§3 now precedes §2 in the file: the real functor `PicSharp` below
consumes `functorial` as its morphism action.)

Blueprint reference: `lem:rel_pic_sharp_functorial` (Kleiman §2,
Defs. `df:aPf` + `df:Pfs`). -/

namespace PicSharp

/-- **Functoriality of the relative Picard presheaf, group-hom form.**

For a base scheme `S`, a curve-side morphism `πC : C ⟶ S`, test objects
`πT : T ⟶ S` and `πT' : T' ⟶ S`, and a morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`
(encoded by `πT' = g ≫ πT`), the set map
`RelPicPresheaf.functorial πC πT πT' g hg` upgrades to an
`AddMonoidHom`-homomorphism with respect to the abelian-group structure
of `PicSharp.addCommGroup` on source and target.

Run-0005 session 0015 (Lane TS D4′ consumption): the body is now
**real** — the zero-`AddMonoidHom` stub is replaced by the genuine
group homomorphism wrapping `RelPicPresheaf.functorial`:

- `map_zero'` — pullback preserves the structure-sheaf class:
  `Modules.pullbackUnitIso` gives `g_C^* 𝒪_{C ×_S T} ≅ 𝒪_{C ×_S T'}`.
- `map_add'` — pullback preserves the tensor-product class:
  `Modules.pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial` (the D4′ loc-triv
  comparison iso, landed with the T1 `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`
  merge-back) gives `g_C^*(L ⊗ L') ≅ g_C^* L ⊗ g_C^* L'` on
  locally-trivial representatives. -/
noncomputable def functorial {S C T T' : Scheme.{u}}
    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) (πT' : T' ⟶ S) (g : T' ⟶ T)
    (hg : πT' = g ≫ πT) :
    Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT) →+
      Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT') where
  toFun := RelPicPresheaf.functorial πC πT πT' g hg
  map_zero' := Quotient.sound ⟨Modules.pullbackUnitIso _⟩
  map_add' a b := by
    induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ L => ?_
    induction b using Quotient.ind with | _ L' => ?_
    exact Quotient.sound
      ⟨Modules.pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial _ L.carrier L'.carrier
        L.isLocallyTrivial L'.isLocallyTrivial⟩

/-- `functorial` at the identity is the identity: `id_C ×_S id_T` is the
identity of `C ×_S T` (`pullback.map_id`), and pullback along the identity
is naturally isomorphic to the identity functor (`Modules.pullbackId`). -/
private lemma functorial_id {S C T : Scheme.{u}}
    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) (hg : πT = 𝟙 T ≫ πT)
    (a : Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT)) :
    functorial πC πT πT (𝟙 T) hg a = a := by
  induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ L => ?_
  refine Quotient.sound ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr ?_).app L.carrier ≪≫
    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId _).app L.carrier⟩
  apply Limits.pullback.hom_ext <;> simp

/-- `functorial` is contravariantly compositional: `(h ≫ g)_C = h_C ≫ g_C`
(`pullback.hom_ext` chase), and pullback along a composite is the composite
of the pullbacks (`Modules.pullbackComp`). -/
private lemma functorial_comp {S C T T' T'' : Scheme.{u}}
    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) (πT' : T' ⟶ S) (πT'' : T'' ⟶ S)
    (g : T' ⟶ T) (h : T'' ⟶ T') (hg : πT' = g ≫ πT) (hh : πT'' = h ≫ πT')
    (hgh : πT'' = (h ≫ g) ≫ πT)
    (a : Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT)) :
    functorial πC πT πT'' (h ≫ g) hgh a
      = functorial πC πT' πT'' h hh (functorial πC πT πT' g hg a) := by
  induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ L => ?_
  refine Quotient.sound ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr ?_).app L.carrier ≪≫
    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp _ _).symm.app L.carrier⟩
  apply Limits.pullback.hom_ext <;> simp

end PicSharp

/-! ## §2. The relative Picard presheaf as a group-valued functor

We assemble the data of A.1.b (object-level quotient set,
`RelPicPresheaf.functorial` morphism action) and §1 (abelian-group
instance on each quotient) into a single contravariant functor

```
PicSharp_{C/k} : (Over (Spec k))^op ⥤ AddCommGrpCat
```

sending an `Spec k`-scheme `T` to the abelian group `Pic(C ×_k T)`
(with the structure of §1; see the §1 note — the present carrier
setoid is the iso-class relation, i.e. the ABSOLUTE Picard group of
the product, pending the `H_T`-coset refinement), and a morphism
`g : T' ⟶ T` over `Spec k` to the group homomorphism
`g^♯ : Pic^♯_{C/k}(T) → Pic^♯_{C/k}(T')` descended from the
line-bundle pullback `g_C^* := (id_C ×_k g)^*`.

Blueprint reference: `def:rel_pic_sharp` (Kleiman §2, Def. `df:Pfs`). -/

/-- The **relative Picard functor** of a smooth proper geometrically
integral curve `C` over a field `k`, as a contravariant functor

```
PicSharp_{C/k} : (Over (Spec k))^op ⥤ AddCommGrpCat
```

On objects: `T ↦ Pic(C ×_k T)`, the tensor-product Picard group of the
product (Lean instance `PicSharp.addCommGroup`; the carrier setoid is
presently the iso-class relation — the ABSOLUTE Picard group — pending
the `H_T`-coset refinement of `preimage_subgroup`, see the §1 note).

On morphisms: `g ↦ g^♯`, the group homomorphism descended from
`g_C^* = (id_C ×_k g)^*` via the quotient (the set-level map is
`RelPicPresheaf.functorial`; the group-hom upgrade is
`PicSharp.functorial` above). Identity and composition laws are
`PicSharp.functorial_id` / `PicSharp.functorial_comp`, i.e.
`Modules.pullbackId` / `Modules.pullbackComp` descended through the
quotient.

Universe: object map values are `AddCommGrpCat.{u+1}` because the
underlying carrier `Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT)` lives in
`Type (u+1)` (since `LineBundle.OnProduct` is defined to land in
`Type (u+1)`).

Run-0005 session 0015 (Lane TS D4′ consumption): the body is now
**real** — the `Functor.const`-at-`PUnit` stub is replaced by the
genuine group-valued assignment
`obj T := AddCommGrpCat.of (Quotient (preimage_subgroup _C.hom T.unop.hom))`
with `map := PicSharp.functorial`, exactly as the stub-era docstring
promised (signature preserved verbatim). The D4′ gate
(`pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial`) landed with the T1
`Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso` merge-back. -/
noncomputable def PicSharp {k : Type u} [Field k] (_C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 _C.hom] [IsProper _C.hom] :
    (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{u+1} where
  obj T := AddCommGrpCat.of
    (Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup _C.hom T.unop.hom))
  map {T T'} g := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom
    (PicSharp.functorial _C.hom T.unop.hom T'.unop.hom g.unop.left
      (Over.w g.unop).symm)
  map_id T := by
    ext a
    exact PicSharp.functorial_id _C.hom T.unop.hom _ a
  map_comp {T T' T''} g h := by
    ext a
    exact PicSharp.functorial_comp _C.hom T.unop.hom T'.unop.hom T''.unop.hom
      g.unop.left h.unop.left (Over.w g.unop).symm (Over.w h.unop).symm _ a

namespace PicSharp

\end{proof}

\section{The relative Picard presheaf as a group-valued presheaf}
\label{sec:relpic_groupvalued}

We now refine the set-valued functor of \cref{thm:pullback_natural} into a
group-valued presheaf.

\begin{definition}\leanok
[Relative Picard presheaf]
  \label{def:rel_pic_sharp}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp}
  \uses{def:line_bundle_on_product, def:pullback_along_projection,
    thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined, lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid,
    thm:pullback_natural}

  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", \S 2 (FGA Explained Ch.~9 \S 9.2),
  % Definition df:Pfs (the relative Picard functor)
  % (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1311-L1318).
  % SOURCE QUOTE:
  % "\begin{dfn}\label{df:Pfs}
  %   The {\it relative Picard functor} \(\Pic_{X/S}\) is defined by
  %     \(\)\Pic_{X/S}(T):=\Pic(X_T)\big/\Pic(T).\(\)
  %  Denote its associated sheaves in the Zariski, \'etale, and fppf
  % topologies  by
  %     \(\)\Pic_{(X/S)\zar},\ \Pic_{(X/S)\et},\
  %     \Pic_{(X/S)\fppf}.\(\)
  %  \end{dfn}"
  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2,
  Def.~df:Pfs.}
  Let \(C/k\) be a smooth proper geometrically integral curve. The
  \emph{relative Picard presheaf} of \(C\) over \(k\) is the functor
  \[
    \Pic^\sharp_{C/k} \;\;\colon\;\; (\Sch/k)^{op} \;\longrightarrow\; \Ab,
    \qquad
    T \;\longmapsto\; \Pic(C \times_k T) \,/\, \pi_T^*\Pic(T),
  \]
  where the right-hand side is the quotient abelian group of
  \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}, and the structure morphisms are induced by
  the pullback maps \(g^\sharp\) of \cref{thm:pullback_natural} (which restrict
  to group homomorphisms on the quotient because the source square commutes
  with all pullback functors, see the proof below). In Kleiman's notation with
  \(X = C\) and \(S = \Spec k\), this is exactly \(\Pic_{X/S}\) of df:Pfs.
\end{definition}

% NOTE (iter-199 plan agent): the Lean closure
% AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp at the pinned Mathlib commit
% b80f227 carries a constant-functor placeholder body
% ((CategoryTheory.Functor.const _).obj (AddCommGrpCat.of PUnit)),
% i.e.\ the constant functor at the trivial abelian group. This is NOT
% the relative Picard presheaf
% \(T \mapsto \Pic(C \times_k T)/\pi_T^*\Pic(T)\) that the definition
% above describes; the placeholder merely supplies a type-checking
% object of the correct kind. The closure is gated on the upstream
% Mathlib \texttt{Scheme.Modules} monoidal-structure gap (no
% tensor-product \texttt{AddCommGroup} structure on
% \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct} is available, hence no quotient group
% can be built on the Lean side until that gap is closed). DO NOT
% promote this block to \leanok via the deterministic \texttt{sync\_leanok}
% phase, and DO NOT mark it formalised in review, until the body is
% replaced with the mathematically correct construction.

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Naturality respects the group structure]
  \label{lem:rel_pic_sharp_functorial}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.functorial}
  \uses{def:rel_pic_sharp, lem:pullback_compose,
    lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid, thm:pullback_natural}

  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", \S 2 (FGA Explained Ch.~9 \S 9.2),
  % Definitions df:aPf + df:Pfs (the relative Picard functor is functorial
  % into abelian groups; the word \emph{functor} into the category of abelian
  % groups encodes both the identity / composition laws and the
  % group-homomorphism property of the restriction maps)
  % (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1274-L1318).
  % SOURCE QUOTE:
  % "The {\it absolute Picard functor} \(\Pic_X\) is the functor from  the
  %  category of (locally Noetherian) \(S\)-schemes \(T\) to the category of
  % abelian groups defined by the formula
  %     \(\)\Pic_X(T):=\Pic(X_T).\(\)
  %  [\dots]
  %  \begin{dfn}\label{df:Pfs}
  %   The {\it relative Picard functor} \(\Pic_{X/S}\) is defined by
  %     \(\)\Pic_{X/S}(T):=\Pic(X_T)\big/\Pic(T).\(\)"
  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2,
  Defs.~df:aPf + df:Pfs (the target category is the category of abelian
  groups; the structure maps are group homomorphisms).}
  For a morphism \(g : T' \to T\) of \(k\)-schemes, the induced set map
  \(g^\sharp : \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T) \to \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T')\) of
  \cref{thm:pullback_natural} is a homomorphism of abelian groups with respect
  to the structure of \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}, and the identity and
  composition laws \(\mathrm{id}^\sharp = \mathrm{id}\) and
  \((g \circ h)^\sharp = h^\sharp \circ g^\sharp\) hold as identities of group
  homomorphisms.
\end{lemma}

% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: Kleiman \S 2 does not write out a separate proof for
% functoriality; the structural assertion is part of the definition. No
% verbatim proof body is available in the source; the project-side argument
% is a direct unfolding of the pullback of modules.
% NOTE (iter-199 plan agent): the Lean closure
% AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.functorial at b80f227 carries a
% zero AddMonoidHom body (\(0 : \dots \to \dots\)), i.e.\ the zero map
% between two trivial-group endpoints, NOT the pullback-descended
% homomorphism this lemma describes. Moreover, because the codomain's
% \texttt{Zero} instance is derived from the sorry-body
% \texttt{addCommGroup} (the file-local genuine sorry of the chapter),
% the closure also inherits \texttt{sorryAx} via that typeclass leak;
% it is therefore not even axiom-clean. The closure is gated on the
% upstream Mathlib \texttt{Scheme.Modules} monoidal-structure gap. DO
% NOT promote this block to \leanok via the deterministic
% \texttt{sync\_leanok} phase, and DO NOT mark it formalised in
% review, until the body is replaced with the mathematically correct
% map descended from \(g_C^*\).
\begin{proof}




\leanok
  By \cref{thm:pullback_natural}, the set map \(g^\sharp\) is defined by
  \([\mathcal{L}] \mapsto [g_C^* \mathcal{L}]\), where
  \(g_C := \mathrm{id}_C \times_k g : C \times_k T' \to C \times_k T\). Pullback
  of \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules along \(g_C\) preserves tensor products and the
  structure sheaf, hence the map
  \(g_C^* : \Pic(C \times_k T) \to \Pic(C \times_k T')\) on Picard groups is
  itself an abelian-group homomorphism. By
  \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}, the quotient map
  \(\Pic(C \times_k T) \to \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T)\) is a group homomorphism, and
  \(g_C^*\) takes \(H_T\) into \(H_{T'}\) by \cref{lem:pullback_compose}; therefore
  \(g^\sharp\) is a group homomorphism on the quotient. The identity and
  composition laws are inherited from \(\mathrm{id}^* = \mathrm{id}\) and
  \((g \circ h)^* = h^* \circ g^*\) on \(\mathcal{O}\)-module pullback.
\end{proof}

\begin{theorem}\leanok

\begin{theorem}\leanok
[\(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}\) is a group-valued presheaf]
  \label{thm:rel_pic_sharp_presheaf}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.presheaf}
  \uses{def:rel_pic_sharp, lem:rel_pic_sharp_functorial,
    lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}

  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", \S 2 (FGA Explained Ch.~9 \S 9.2),
  % Definitions df:aPf + df:Pfs (relative Picard functor as a functor into
  % abelian groups)
  % (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1274-L1318).
  % SOURCE QUOTE:
  % "The {\it absolute Picard functor} \(\Pic_X\) is the functor from  the
  %  category of (locally Noetherian) \(S\)-schemes \(T\) to the category of
  % abelian groups defined by the formula
  %     \(\)\Pic_X(T):=\Pic(X_T).\(\)"
  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2,
  Defs.~df:aPf + df:Pfs.}
  The assignment \(T \mapsto \Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T)\) of \cref{def:rel_pic_sharp},
  together with the structure maps \(g \mapsto g^\sharp\) of
  \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_functorial}, defines a presheaf of abelian groups
  \[
    \Pic^\sharp_{C/k} \;\;\colon\;\; (\Sch/k)^{op} \;\longrightarrow\;
                                     \AddCommGroup
  \]
  on the category of \(k\)-schemes.
\end{theorem}

% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: As with the prior theorems, Kleiman \S 2 does not supply
% a separate proof body --- the presheaf-of-abelian-groups property is part of
% the definition df:aPf + df:Pfs. The project-side proof assembles the lemmas
% above; no external proof is being translated.
% NOTE (iter-199 plan agent): the Lean closure
% AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.presheaf at b80f227 is defined as
% \texttt{PicSharp \_C}, i.e.\ it re-exports the constant-PUnit-functor
% placeholder from \texttt{PicSharp} above; it is NOT the canonical
% presheaf assembled from the pullback-descended functorial action
% \(g \mapsto g^\sharp\) that this theorem describes. The closure is
% gated on the upstream Mathlib \texttt{Scheme.Modules}
% monoidal-structure gap. DO NOT promote this block to \leanok via the
% deterministic \texttt{sync\_leanok} phase, and DO NOT mark it
% formalised in review, until the body is replaced with the
% mathematically correct presheaf assembly.
\begin{proof}




\leanok
  The object map is well defined by
  \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}: for each \(k\)-scheme \(T\), the value
  \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T)\) is an abelian group. The morphism map
  \(g \mapsto g^\sharp\) is a group homomorphism by
  \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_functorial}, and satisfies the identity and
  composition laws by the same lemma. These data assemble into a functor
  \((\Sch/k)^{op} \to \AddCommGroup\), i.e.\ a presheaf of abelian groups on
  \((\Sch/k)\).
\end{proof}

\section{\'Etale sheafification}
\label{sec:relpic_etale_sheaf}

The presheaf \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}\) is generally not an \'etale (or even Zariski)
sheaf. The standard obstruction (Kleiman \S 2, L1292--L1302): for a \(k\)-scheme
\(T\) carrying a non-trivial line bundle whose pullback to \(C \times_k T\) becomes
non-trivial after an open cover, the Zariski separated-presheaf condition
fails. Therefore, to obtain a representable functor in the sense of
\cref{chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability}, one must replace
\(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}\) by its \emph{associated \'etale sheaf}. Kleiman \S 2 names
this sheaf \(\Pic_{(X/S)\et}\) and singles it out as the functor that Kleiman's
main existence theorem in \S 4 represents.

% this auxiliary statement --- it is the standard fact that the sheafification
% of a presheaf of abelian groups in a Grothendieck topology is again a
% presheaf of abelian groups (equivalently, the forgetful functor
% \(\AddCommGroup \to \Set\) preserves filtered colimits, and the
% plus-construction is built from such colimits). The Lean side appeals to
% Mathlib's \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Sheafification}\) machinery.

\begin{proof}




\leanok
  The canonical morphism from a presheaf to its associated sheaf in
  any Grothendieck topology (Stacks tag 00WI; the canonical
  sheafification unit) supplies, in particular, a morphism of
  presheaves \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k} \to \Pic^\sharp_{(C/k)\et}\); the
  hom-set of presheaf morphisms between these two objects is therefore
  nonempty. The full universal property of this canonical morphism

  the sheafification is uniquely pinned by it --- is the content of
  the forward-looking \cref{thm:rel_pic_etale_sheaf_unit_canonical}
  below; see its proof sketch for the canonical formulation.
\end{proof}

text: The rewire looks feasible. Let me check FGA's imports and the representability/group-structure sections for universe-sensitive uses.

input.description: Read FGA imports and representability section

data.content:
- §4 Lem. `lm:qt` (smooth-proper quotient of étale sheaves).
-/

set_option autoImplicit false

universe u

open CategoryTheory Limits

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

namespace Scheme

namespace PicScheme

/-! ## §0. File-internal placeholders for the sibling-chapter functors
6:import Mathlib
===
quasi-projective `k`-subschemes. The representing scheme is the **Picard
scheme** `Pic_{C/k}` of `PicScheme` above.

Blueprint reference: `thm:fga_pic_representability` (Kleiman §4 Thm.
`th:main` + Cor. `cor:algsch`). -/

/-- Typeclass asserting that `picSharp C` is representable by `PicScheme C`.
The single `⟨sorry⟩` instance below is the unique sorry-carrying site for
`representable`. -/
class PicSharpRepresentable {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] : Prop where
  has_representable : Nonempty ((picSharp C).RepresentableBy (PicScheme C))

/-- **FGA representability of the Picard scheme.**

Let `k` be a field and `C` a smooth proper geometrically integral curve
over `k`. Then the étale-sheafified relative Picard functor `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`
is representable by the Picard scheme `PicScheme C`.

iter-177+: the body assembles the four-step Kleiman §4 Thm. `th:main` proof:
1. Stratify `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}` by Hilbert polynomial into a disjoint open
   cover `(P^φ)_φ`.
2. For each `φ`, bound twists by `m`-regularity to reduce to `P^{φ_0}_0`.
3. Factor through the Abel map `A_{C/k}` of `abelMap` to obtain
   `Z := P^{φ_0}_0 ×_{Pic^♯} Div_{C/k}`, a quasi-projective open of
   `Div_{C/k}`.
4. Apply `smoothProperQuotient` to the projection `Z ⟶ P^{φ_0}_0` to
   represent each `P^{φ_0}_0`; reassemble over `φ` for the full
   `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`.

The body is extracted from `PicSharpRepresentable.has_representable` via
`Classical.choice`. -/
noncomputable def representable {k : Type u} [Field k]
    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] [PicSharpRepresentable C] :
    (picSharp C).RepresentableBy (PicScheme C) :=
  Classical.choice (PicSharpRepresentable.has_representable (C := C))

/-- Existence instance for `PicSharpRepresentable`. This is the single
sorry-carrying site for `representable`. -/
noncomputable instance instPicSharpRepresentable {k : Type u} [Field k]
    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] : PicSharpRepresentable C :=
  ⟨sorry⟩

/-! ## §5. The abelian group-scheme structure

The Picard scheme `Pic_{C/k}` inherits an abelian group-scheme structure
from the tensor product of invertible sheaves on `C ×_k T`. The relative
Picard presheaf `T ↦ Pic(C ×_k T)/π_T^* Pic(T)` is already an abelian
group at every `T` (the quotient of an abelian group by a subgroup),
functorially in `T`; this lifts to the étale sheafification (sheafification
of an abelian-presheaf is an abelian-sheaf, since it commutes with finite
products in the value category), and then to a group-scheme structure on
the representing scheme by Yoneda.

Blueprint reference: `thm:pic_is_group_scheme` (Kleiman §2 Def. `df:Pfs` +
§4 Def. `df:Psch`). -/

/-- Typeclass asserting that `PicScheme C` carries a `GrpObj` structure.
The single `⟨sorry⟩` instance below is the unique sorry-carrying site for
`groupSchemeStructure`. -/
class PicSchemeGroupObject {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] : Prop where
  has_group_object : Nonempty (GrpObj (PicScheme C))

/-- **The Picard scheme is a `k`-group scheme.**

The Picard scheme `PicScheme C` carries a canonical `GrpObj` structure (a
group-object structure in `Over (Spec (.of k))`), obtained by Yoneda
transport of the abelian-group structure on `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}(T)` (sum:
`[L] + [M] = [L ⊗ M]`; inverse: `-[L] = [L⁻¹]`; unit: `[O_{C ×_k T}]`).

The full abelian-group-scheme refinement is the conjunction of this `GrpObj`
instance with the `IsCommMonObj (PicScheme C)` predicate (commutativity of
the multiplication), which holds because tensor product of invertible
sheaves is symmetric; `IsCommMonObj` is a separate instance threaded on top
of `GrpObj` in iter-177+.

iter-177+: the body invokes the Yoneda lemma to transport the natural
abelian-group structure on `T ↦ picSharp C |_T` to the multiplication /
inverse / unit morphisms on `PicScheme C`; the group axioms hold at the
representable level functorially in `T`, hence on the representing scheme
by Yoneda. The body is extracted from `PicSchemeGroupObject.has_group_object`
via `Classical.choice`. -/
noncomputable instance groupSchemeStructure {k : Type u} [Field k]
    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] [PicSchemeGroupObject C] :
    GrpObj (PicScheme C) :=
  Classical.choice (PicSchemeGroupObject.has_group_object (C := C))

/-- Existence instance for `PicSchemeGroupObject`. This is the single
sorry-carrying site for `groupSchemeStructure`. -/
noncomputable instance instPicSchemeGroupObject {k : Type u} [Field k]
    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] : PicSchemeGroupObject C :=
  ⟨sorry⟩

end PicScheme

end Scheme

end AlgebraicGeometry

483	**Relation (multiplicative form).** For `L L' : OnProduct πC πT` set
484	```
485	L ~ L'   ↔   ∃ N locally trivial on T,  L.carrier ≅ π_T^* N ⊗ L'.carrier,
486	```
487	where `π_T^* N = (LineBundle.pullbackAlongProjection πC πT N hN).carrier`. This is the left-coset
488	relation of `H_T := im π_T^*`. It is **equivalent** to the blueprint's `L ⊗ L'^{-1} ≅ π_T^* N`
489	(tensor both sides by `L'` / its inverse), but the multiplicative form avoids naming the tensor
490	inverse of `L'` in the *definition* — only the setoid-symmetry step consumes a tensor inverse (of
491	`N` on the base `T`, via `Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse`). NOTE FOR REVIEW: the blueprint pins
492	`lem:relpic_setoid_{refl,symm,trans}`, `lem:relpic_add_welldef`, `lem:pullback_inverse_iso` are
493	realized below under the multiplicative form; the `\lean{...TODO...}` pins should be repinned to
494	`relPicSetoid`/`relPicRel_refl`/`relPicRel_symm`/`relPicRel_trans`/`relPicRel_add` and the relation
495	in the chapter noted as the multiplicative form.
496	
497	These declarations consume the SAME now sorry-free `Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse`
498	reverse bridge `IsLocallyTrivial ⟹ IsInvertible` (also used by the absolute
499	`addCommGroup`); no file-local `sorry` is introduced. -/

501	/-- **The `H_T`-coset relation on `OnProduct πC πT`** (relative Picard, multiplicative form):
502	`L ~ L' ↔ ∃ N loc-triv on T, L.carrier ≅ π_T^* N ⊗ L'.carrier`. Project-local: encodes the
503	quotient `Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)` of `lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid`, coarser than the absolute
504	iso-class relation `RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup`. -/
505	def relPicRel {S C T : Scheme.{u}} (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S)
506	    (L L' : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT) : Prop :=
507	  ∃ (N : T.Modules) (hN : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial N),
508	    Nonempty (L.carrier ≅
509	      Modules.tensorObj (LineBundle.pullbackAlongProjection πC πT N hN).carrier L'.carrier)
510	
511	/-- An isomorphism of the underlying bundles implies the `H_T`-coset relation (take `N = 𝒪_T`):
512	the relative relation is coarser than the absolute iso-class relation. Project-local bridge used to
513	transport the abelian-group axioms from the absolute iso-class group. -/
514	theorem relPicRel_of_iso {S C T : Scheme.{u}} {πC : C ⟶ S} {πT : T ⟶ S}
515	    {L L' : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT} (e : Nonempty (L.carrier ≅ L'.carrier)) :
516	    relPicRel πC πT L L' := by
517	  obtain ⟨e⟩ := e
518	  refine ⟨SheafOfModules.unit T.ringCatSheaf, isLocallyTrivial_unit, ⟨?_⟩⟩
519	  exact e ≪≫ (Modules.tensorObj_left_unitor L'.carrier).symm ≪≫
520	    Modules.tensorObjIsoOfIso (Modules.pullbackUnitIso (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT)).symm
521	      (Iso.refl L'.carrier)
522	
523	/-- **Reflexivity of the `H_T`-relation** (blueprint `lem:relpic_setoid_refl`): `L ~ L`, witnessed
524	by `N = 𝒪_T` and the pullback–unit iso `π_T^* 𝒪_T ≅ 𝒪_{C×T}` plus the left unitor. -/
525	theorem relPicRel_refl {S C T : Scheme.{u}} (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S)
526	    (L : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT) : relPicRel πC πT L L :=
527	  relPicRel_of_iso ⟨Iso.refl _⟩
528	
529	/-- **Symmetry of the `H_T`-relation** (blueprint `lem:relpic_setoid_symm`): from `L ~ L'` (via `N`)
530	get `L' ~ L` (via the tensor inverse `N⁻¹` on `T`), using seed-1 multiplicativity of `π_T^*`, the
531	pullback–unit iso, and `Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse`. -/
532	theorem relPicRel_symm {S C T : Scheme.{u}} {πC : C ⟶ S} {πT : T ⟶ S}
533	    {L L' : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT} (h : relPicRel πC πT L L') :
534	    relPicRel πC πT L' L := by
535	  obtain ⟨N, hN, ⟨e⟩⟩ := h
536	  obtain ⟨Ninv, hNinv, ⟨eN⟩⟩ := Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse hN
537	  refine ⟨Ninv, hNinv, ⟨?_⟩⟩
538	  -- `tensorObj (π_T^*Ninv) L ≅ L'`, then take `.symm`.
539	  refine Iso.symm ?_
540	  refine Modules.tensorObjIsoOfIso (Iso.refl _) e ≪≫ ?_
541	  refine (Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso
542	    (M := (LineBundle.pullbackAlongProjection πC πT Ninv hNinv).carrier)
543	    (N := (LineBundle.pullbackAlongProjection πC πT N hN).carrier) (P := L'.carrier)).symm ≪≫ ?_
544	  refine Modules.tensorObjIsoOfIso
545	    (Modules.pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT) Ninv N hNinv hN).symm
546	    (Iso.refl L'.carrier) ≪≫ ?_
547	  refine Modules.tensorObjIsoOfIso
548	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT)).mapIso
549	      (Modules.tensorObj_braiding Ninv N ≪≫ eN)) (Iso.refl L'.carrier) ≪≫ ?_
550	  exact Modules.tensorObjIsoOfIso (Modules.pullbackUnitIso (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT))
551	    (Iso.refl L'.carrier) ≪≫ Modules.tensorObj_left_unitor L'.carrier
552	
553	/-- **Transitivity of the `H_T`-relation** (blueprint `lem:relpic_setoid_trans`): from `L ~ L'` (via
554	`N`) and `L' ~ L''` (via `N'`) get `L ~ L''` via `N ⊗ N'`, using the associator and seed-1
555	multiplicativity of `π_T^*`. -/
556	theorem relPicRel_trans {S C T : Scheme.{u}} {πC : C ⟶ S} {πT : T ⟶ S}
557	    {L L' L'' : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT}
558	    (h : relPicRel πC πT L L') (h' : relPicRel πC πT L' L'') :
559	    relPicRel πC πT L L'' := by
560	  obtain ⟨N, hN, ⟨e⟩⟩ := h
561	  obtain ⟨N', hN', ⟨e'⟩⟩ := h'
562	  refine ⟨Modules.tensorObj N N', Modules.tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial hN hN', ⟨?_⟩⟩
563	  refine e ≪≫ Modules.tensorObjIsoOfIso (Iso.refl _) e' ≪≫ ?_
564	  refine (Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso
565	    (M := (LineBundle.pullbackAlongProjection πC πT N hN).carrier)
566	    (N := (LineBundle.pullbackAlongProjection πC πT N' hN').carrier) (P := L''.carrier)).symm ≪≫ ?_
567	  exact Modules.tensorObjIsoOfIso
568	    (Modules.pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT) N N' hN hN').symm
569	    (Iso.refl L''.carrier)
570	
571	/-- **The relative Picard carrier setoid** `Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)` (blueprint
572	`lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid`, carrier), the `H_T`-coset relation on `LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT`.
573	Project-local: this is the RELATIVE quotient the seed names, distinct from the absolute iso-class
574	setoid `RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup`. -/
575	def relPicSetoid {S C T : Scheme.{u}} (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) :
576	    Setoid (LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT) where
577	  r := relPicRel πC πT
578	  iseqv := ⟨relPicRel_refl πC πT, relPicRel_symm, relPicRel_trans⟩
579	
580	/-- **The middle-four interchange for `⊗`** `(A ⊗ B) ⊗ (C ⊗ D) ≅ (A ⊗ C) ⊗ (B ⊗ D)`, assembled from
581	the associator and the braiding. Project-local helper for `relPicRel_add`. -/
582	private noncomputable def tensorMiddleFour {X : Scheme.{u}} (A B C D : X.Modules) :
583	    Modules.tensorObj (Modules.tensorObj A B) (Modules.tensorObj C D) ≅
584	      Modules.tensorObj (Modules.tensorObj A C) (Modules.tensorObj B D) :=
585	  Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso ≪≫
586	    Modules.tensorObjIsoOfIso (Iso.refl A)
587	      (Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso (M := B) (N := C) (P := D)).symm ≪≫
588	    Modules.tensorObjIsoOfIso (Iso.refl A)
589	      (Modules.tensorObjIsoOfIso (Modules.tensorObj_braiding B C) (Iso.refl D)) ≪≫
590	    Modules.tensorObjIsoOfIso (Iso.refl A)
591	      (Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso (M := C) (N := B) (P := D)) ≪≫
592	    (Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso (M := A) (N := C) (P := Modules.tensorObj B D)).symm
593	
594	/-- **Addition is well defined on the `H_T`-quotient** (blueprint `lem:relpic_add_welldef`): if
595	`L₁ ~ L₂` and `L₁' ~ L₂'` then `L₁ ⊗ L₁' ~ L₂ ⊗ L₂'`, via the middle-four interchange and seed-1
596	multiplicativity of `π_T^*`. -/
597	theorem relPicRel_add {S C T : Scheme.{u}} {πC : C ⟶ S} {πT : T ⟶ S}
598	    {L₁ L₂ L₁' L₂' : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT}
599	    (h : relPicRel πC πT L₁ L₂) (h' : relPicRel πC πT L₁' L₂') :
600	    relPicRel πC πT (Modules.tensorObjOnProduct πC πT L₁ L₁')
601	      (Modules.tensorObjOnProduct πC πT L₂ L₂') := by
602	  obtain ⟨N, hN, ⟨e⟩⟩ := h
603	  obtain ⟨N', hN', ⟨e'⟩⟩ := h'
604	  refine ⟨Modules.tensorObj N N', Modules.tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial hN hN', ⟨?_⟩⟩
605	  -- `L₁ ⊗ L₁' ≅ (π_T^*N ⊗ L₂) ⊗ (π_T^*N' ⊗ L₂')`.
606	  refine Modules.tensorObjIsoOfIso e e' ≪≫ ?_
607	  -- middle-four → `(π_T^*N ⊗ π_T^*N') ⊗ (L₂ ⊗ L₂')`.
608	  refine tensorMiddleFour (LineBundle.pullbackAlongProjection πC πT N hN).carrier L₂.carrier
609	    (LineBundle.pullbackAlongProjection πC πT N' hN').carrier L₂'.carrier ≪≫ ?_
610	  -- seed-1: `π_T^*N ⊗ π_T^*N' ≅ π_T^*(N ⊗ N')`.
611	  exact Modules.tensorObjIsoOfIso
612	    (Modules.pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT) N N' hN hN').symm
613	    (Iso.refl (Modules.tensorObj L₂.carrier L₂'.carrier))
614	
615	/-- The chosen tensor-inverse of `L` as an element of `OnProduct πC πT`, via
616	the now sorry-free `Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse`. Project-local:
617	underlies negation on the relative Picard quotient. -/
618	private noncomputable def relNegOnProduct {S C T : Scheme.{u}} {πC : C ⟶ S} {πT : T ⟶ S}
619	    (L : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT) : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT :=
620	  ⟨Classical.choose (Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse L.isLocallyTrivial),
621	    (Classical.choose_spec (Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse L.isLocallyTrivial)).1⟩
622	
623	/-- **Negation is compatible with the `H_T`-relation** (blueprint `lem:relpic_setoid_symm`, negation
624	form): if `L ~ M` then their chosen tensor-inverses satisfy `L⁻¹ ~ M⁻¹`. The witness is `N⁻¹` on the
625	base `T`; both inverses are tensor-inverses of `L`, so they agree up to iso by `pInverseUnique`. -/
626	theorem relPicRel_neg {S C T : Scheme.{u}} {πC : C ⟶ S} {πT : T ⟶ S}
627	    {L M : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT} (h : relPicRel πC πT L M) :
628	    relPicRel πC πT (relNegOnProduct L) (relNegOnProduct M) := by
629	  obtain ⟨N, hN, ⟨e⟩⟩ := h
630	  obtain ⟨Ninv, hNinv, ⟨eN⟩⟩ := Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse hN
631	  refine ⟨Ninv, hNinv, ?_⟩
632	  have hLinv : Modules.tensorObj L.carrier (relNegOnProduct L).carrier ≅
633	      SheafOfModules.unit (Limits.pullback πC πT).ringCatSheaf :=
634	    (Classical.choose_spec (Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse L.isLocallyTrivial)).2.some
635	  have hMinv : Modules.tensorObj M.carrier (relNegOnProduct M).carrier ≅
636	      SheafOfModules.unit (Limits.pullback πC πT).ringCatSheaf :=
637	    (Classical.choose_spec (Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse M.isLocallyTrivial)).2.some
638	  have hOther : Modules.tensorObj L.carrier
639	      (Modules.tensorObj (LineBundle.pullbackAlongProjection πC πT Ninv hNinv).carrier
640	        (relNegOnProduct M).carrier) ≅
641	      SheafOfModules.unit (Limits.pullback πC πT).ringCatSheaf := by
642	    refine Modules.tensorObjIsoOfIso e (Iso.refl _) ≪≫ ?_
643	    refine tensorMiddleFour (LineBundle.pullbackAlongProjection πC πT N hN).carrier M.carrier
644	      (LineBundle.pullbackAlongProjection πC πT Ninv hNinv).carrier
645	      (relNegOnProduct M).carrier ≪≫ ?_
646	    refine Modules.tensorObjIsoOfIso
647	      (Modules.pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial
648	        (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT) N Ninv hN hNinv).symm hMinv ≪≫ ?_
649	    refine Modules.tensorObjIsoOfIso
650	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT)).mapIso eN) (Iso.refl _) ≪≫ ?_
651	    refine Modules.tensorObjIsoOfIso (Modules.pullbackUnitIso (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT))
652	      (Iso.refl _) ≪≫ ?_
653	    exact Modules.tensorObj_left_unitor _
654	  exact pInverseUnique hLinv hOther
